table.name: Ecdlp.NormEDS.normEDS_shift_mul_shift_of_eq_zero
table.exact_type:
theorem normEDS_shift_mul_shift_of_eq_zero [CommRing R] (b c d : R) {ρ : ℤ}
    (hρ : normEDS b c d ρ = 0) (n : ℤ) :
    normEDS b c d (n + ρ) * normEDS b c d (n - ρ)
      = -(normEDS b c d (ρ + 1) * normEDS b c d (ρ - 1)) * normEDS b c d n ^ 2
hypotheses: R a CommRing (NO domain hypothesis needed)
hρ : normEDS b c d ρ = 0
no nondegeneracy on b, c, d whatsoever
ρ, n arbitrary in ℤ (ρ need not be positive or minimal)
table.allowed_interpretation: For any b,c,d in any commutative ring, at any zero position ρ of W = normEDS b c d, the r = 1 slice of the elliptic relation collapses to the translation identity W(n+ρ)·W(n−ρ) = −W(ρ+1)·W(ρ−1)·W(n)² for all n. Pure algebra downstream of normEDS_isEllSequence (ported Mathlib PR #13155 proof in NormEDSIsElliptic.lean).
does_not_follow: That zeros propagate: without a domain and without W(ρ±1) ≠ 0 nothing about W(n±ρ) individually follows
Anything about minimality or rank of apparition
Anything about curves, polynomials, or torsion
depends_on: normEDS_isEllSequence (Ecdlp/Proved/NormEDSIsElliptic.lean, port of mathlib4 PR #13155 by Junyan Xu / David Angdinata)
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hρ (W ρ = 0): the identity is false in general — it is exactly the specialization of Rel₃ that uses W(ρ) = 0 to kill the left-hand W(ρ)² term.

table.name: Ecdlp.NormEDS.normEDS_sub_eq_zero_of_eq_zero
table.exact_type:
theorem normEDS_sub_eq_zero_of_eq_zero [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] (b c d : R) {ρ : ℤ}
    (hρ : normEDS b c d ρ = 0) (hA : normEDS b c d (ρ + 1) ≠ 0)
    (hB : normEDS b c d (ρ - 1) ≠ 0) {k : ℤ} (hk : normEDS b c d k = 0) :
    normEDS b c d (k - ρ) = 0
hypotheses: IsDomain R (essential: proof splits a triple product = 0)
hρ : W ρ = 0
hk : W k = 0
table.allowed_interpretation: Descent step of Ward's rank-of-apparition argument: a zero at ρ with both neighbours nonzero pulls every other zero down by ρ (the square W(k−ρ)² in the translation identity removes the usual ± case split). Valid for arbitrary ρ, k in ℤ.
does_not_follow: That the neighbour conditions hold for the minimal zero (that is a separate case analysis, Case B of no_consec_aux)
Divisibility ρ ∣ k directly (needs the induction in no_consec_aux)
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop IsDomain: in a ring with zero divisors the product W(ρ+1)·W(ρ−1)·W(k−ρ)² = 0 does not force any factor to vanish. Drop hA: b = c = 0 gives ρ = 2 a zero with W 3 = 0, and zeros are no longer confined to ρℤ.

table.name: Ecdlp.NormEDS.no_consec_aux (private — included because it carries the whole Ward argument)
hypotheses: IsDomain R
N : ℕ (nonnegative index only; negative case handled by reflection in the public theorem)
table.allowed_interpretation: ℕ-indexed core: chooses the minimal positive zero ρ via Nat.sInf, shows ρ ≥ 2 (W 1 = 1), then Case A (W(ρ+1) ≠ 0: every zero index divisible by ρ, so consecutive zeros force ρ ∣ 1) and Case B (W(ρ+1) = 0: ρ = 2 ⇒ b = c = 0 contradicting h23; ρ = 3 ⇒ c = d = 0 contradicting h34 using W 2 = b ≠ 0 by minimality; ρ ≥ 4 self-contradictory via the translation identity plus normEDS_odd). N = 0 is killed separately by W 1 = 1, so the always-present zero W 0 = 0 never counts as half of a violating pair.
does_not_follow: Existence of any zero
The full rank-of-apparition theorem (zeros = ρℤ) as a public statement — the divisibility structure is internal to Case A only
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop h23: (b,c,d) = (0,0,d) has W 2 = b = 0 and W 3 = c = 0, consecutive zeros at (2,3). Drop h34: (b,0,0) with b ≠ 0 has W 3 = c = 0 and W 4 = d·b = 0, consecutive zeros at (3,4). Both stated as sharp in the docstring but NOT formalized as Lean counterexample lemmas.
prose_issues: The module docstring calls the hypotheses 'sharp' and exhibits the counterexamples, but sharpness is only prose + the Python script (and the (0,0,d) case is only a comment there, never executed)

table.name: Ecdlp.NormEDS.normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros
table.exact_type:
theorem normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] (b c d : R)
    (h23 : ¬(b = 0 ∧ c = 0)) (h34 : ¬(c = 0 ∧ d = 0)) (n : ℤ) :
    ¬(normEDS b c d n = 0 ∧ normEDS b c d (n + 1) = 0)
hypotheses: IsDomain R (a bare integral domain — no field, no characteristic hypothesis, char p allowed including char 2/3)
n : ℤ arbitrary — negative n handled by the normEDS_neg reflection
table.allowed_interpretation: Ward apparition rigidity, abstract scalar form: over any integral domain, under the two (sharp) nondegeneracy conditions, normEDS b c d never vanishes at two consecutive integers. Covers all n in ℤ; the pair (n, n+1) = (0, 1) is consistent because W 1 = 1 ≠ 0.
does_not_follow: Any statement about elliptic curves, division polynomials, or torsion (no curve appears in the type)
That the b, c, d hypotheses hold for any particular curve data (discharged separately by the Bézout certificates in DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean)
The classical Ward theorem that the zero set is exactly ρℤ (only the no-consecutive-zeros consequence is public)
Sharpness of h23/h34 as a formal statement
table.counterexample_if_dropped: h23: (0,0,d) zeroes W 2, W 3. h34: (b,0,0) zeroes W 3, W 4. IsDomain: zero-divisor rings break the descent products.
prose_issues: Docstring says 'both necessary' — true but not formalized

Docstring says identities are 'numerically cross-checked over 𝔽_p' by the script; the script uses only p = 10007, positive indices < 320, and never samples b = 0 (its even recurrence divides by b), so the numeric check covers a strict subset of the theorem's domain — acceptable since it is explicitly design insurance, nothing enters the proof

table.name: Ecdlp.NormEDS.normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros'
table.exact_type:
theorem normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros' [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] (b c d : R)
    (h23 : ¬(b = 0 ∧ c = 0)) (h34 : ¬(c = 0 ∧ d = 0)) (n : ℤ) :
    ¬(normEDS b c d n = 0 ∧ normEDS b c d (n - 1) = 0)
hypotheses: Identical to normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros; pair reindexed to (n−1, n)
table.allowed_interpretation: Trivial reindexing of the main theorem to match the exact disjunction shape produced by the evaluation bridge (w n = 0 ∧ (w (n−1) = 0 ∨ w (n+1) = 0)).
does_not_follow: Nothing beyond the main theorem

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃ :
    IsCoprime secp256k1Bar.Ψ₂Sq secp256k1Bar.Ψ₃
hypotheses: None visible; secp256k1Bar := secp256k1.map (algebraMap (ZMod Secp256k1.p) (AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p)))
table.allowed_interpretation: The F_p Bézout certificate secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃ (CoprimePsi2Psi3.lean, native_decide-checked) transported to 𝔽̄_p[X] via IsCoprime.map (mapRingHom) and Mathlib's map_Ψ₂Sq/map_Ψ₃ naturality. Hence Ψ₂Sq and Ψ₃ of secp256k1 have no common root in the algebraic closure.
does_not_follow: Point-level disjointness E[2] ∩ E[3] = {O} (no roots→points transition here)
Anything about y-coordinates

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Ψ₃_preΨ₄
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Ψ₃_preΨ₄ :
    IsCoprime secp256k1Bar.Ψ₃ secp256k1Bar.preΨ₄
hypotheses: None visible
table.allowed_interpretation: Transport of the F_p certificate secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₃_preΨ₄ (CoprimePsi3PrePsi4.lean, native_decide-checked) to 𝔽̄_p[X]; Ψ₃ and preΨ₄ share no root in the closure.
does_not_follow: Point-level statement about E[3] vs E[4]

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq (n : ℤ) :
    IsCoprime (secp256k1.Φ n) (secp256k1.ΨSq n)
hypotheses: n : ℤ completely arbitrary — genuinely covers n = 0, negative n, and n ≡ 0 (mod p); NO characteristic hypothesis, no char ∤ n, no n ≠ 0
No [Fact p.Prime] visible on the statement: ZMod Secp256k1.p is a field via the concrete prime, and the proof route goes through Field/IsAlgClosed instances of the closure
table.allowed_interpretation: Headline N5: for every integer n, the multiplication-by-n numerator Φ n and denominator ΨSq n of secp256k1 are coprime in 𝔽_p[X], i.e. the x-coordinate of [n] as a rational function Φ n / ΨSq n is in lowest terms; equivalently (via the Bézout witnesses evaluating to 1) they share no common root in any field extension. Edge cases: n = 0 is trivially true (Mathlib Φ 0 = 1, ΨSq 0 = 0, and IsCoprime 1 0 holds — content-free); negative n reduces to |n| by evenness of Φ/ΨSq; n a multiple of p is a genuine, non-vacuous instance — the proof is n-uniform because the two certificate inputs are n-independent curve facts, so no degree bookkeeping of ψ_p enters. Proof: contrapositive through exists_common_root_of_not_isCoprime (common root x₀ in 𝔽̄_p), the evaluation bridge (consecutive normEDS zeros at x₀), Ward rigidity (NormEDSConsecutiveZeros), with the two nondegeneracy escapes (β = Ψ₂Sq(x₀) = 0 with Ψ₃(x₀) = 0; Ψ₃(x₀) = 0 with preΨ₄(x₀) = 0) closed by the two transported Bézout certificates.
does_not_follow: #E[n](𝔽̄_p) = n² or any torsion cardinality (the counting half N10(i) is NOT here)
That roots of ΨSq n are exactly x-coordinates of nonzero n-torsion points (roots→points transition is elsewhere/unproved at this layer)
Squarefreeness or separability of ΨSq n / Φ n (separate files DivisionPolynomialSquarefree/Separable)

For p ∣ n, any classical-degree statement about ψ_n: in char p the degree of ψ_p collapses; coprimality survives but its torsion-counting meaning weakens (E[p] of an ordinary curve is ℤ/p, not (ℤ/p)²) — the theorem makes no such claim
IsCoprime (Ψ m) (Ψ n) for m ≠ n
Anything about the point at infinity or y-coordinates
table.counterexample_if_dropped: None to drop — the statement is hypothesis-free in n. Note the domain/closure instances are irreplaceable: the descent needs IsAlgClosed for the square root β of Ψ₂Sq(x₀) and IsDomain for Ward rigidity.
prose_issues: Docstring/file header: '(In char p the classical degree of ψ_p collapses, but the coprimality survives — Φ n stays monic, and this is exactly what the lowest-terms degree bookkeeping of N10(i) consumes.)' — Φ-monic is proved elsewhere (secp256k1_Φ_monic), and 'exactly what N10(i) consumes' is roadmap prose, not established

Header 'No native_decide in this file (the certificates carry their own)' is literally true but the theorem transitively rests on 10+ native_decide residue checks in CoprimePsi2Psi3/CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 plus native_decide facts in Secp256k1Curve — compiler trust is inherited

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq (n : ℤ) :
    IsCoprime (secp256k1Bar.Φ n) (secp256k1Bar.ΨSq n)
hypotheses: n : ℤ arbitrary
table.allowed_interpretation: The F_p coprimality mapped up to 𝔽̄_p[X] via IsCoprime.map and map_Φ/map_ΨSq naturality — the form the announced N10(i) degree bookkeeping would consume.
does_not_follow: Same exclusions as the F_p version; in particular no root counting and no point counting

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_preΨ₇
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_preΨ₇ :
    IsCoprime secp256k1.Ψ₂Sq (secp256k1.preΨ' 7)
hypotheses: None; specific curve, specific n = 7. Nine ZMod p residue identities each discharged by native_decide; Bézout witnesses are 9 private pasted constants U₂₁…U₀, V₀ (CAS-generated, script claims the whole file is machine-emitted)
table.allowed_interpretation: Explicit Bézout identity u·Ψ₂Sq + v·(preΨ' 7) = 1 in 𝔽_p[X] with u sparse on X^{3k} (degree 21) and v constant, validated by collapsing the product onto nine powers of X and checking nine ZMod p residue equations with native_decide. Consequence available (not stated here): Ψ₂Sq = 4X³ + 28 and the degree-24 preΨ' 7 share no root in any extension of 𝔽_p. Verified the collapse: the two concrete polynomials in the key identity match secp256k1_Ψ₂Sq (DivisionPolynomial.lean) and secp256k1_preΨ₇ (CoprimePsi3Psi7.lean) coefficient-for-coefficient (7, 27608, −2101904, −284585728, −2228742656, −26142548992, −330576748544, −661153497088, 377801998336).
does_not_follow: 'E[2] ⊥ E[7]' / 'no nonidentity point is simultaneously 2-torsion and 7-torsion' — that is the docstring's point-level reading; the theorem is polynomial coprimality only, and the roots→points transition (root of preΨ' 7 ↔ x-coordinate of a nonzero 7-torsion point) is asserted in prose, not proved in this file
'y ≠ 0 at every root of preΨ₇' — needs the curve equation plus Ψ₂Sq(x) = 4y² and char ≠ 2 (4 invertible); implicit for this p, proved (if at all) only downstream in SevenTorsionStructure.lean
#E[7](𝔽̄_p) = 49 (a downstream target this feeds, per the docstring)
That preΨ' 7 equals the classical ψ₇ as a function on points (needs the odd-index Ψ = preΨ' identification, from Mathlib conventions, not restated here)
The order-divides-gcd(2,7)=1 argument in the docstring — that is motivation, not the mechanism of the proof
depends_on: generator/validator script scripts/certs/psi2_psi7_coprime_cert.py (prints CERT_OK; checks Bézout product, sparsity, independent recurrence rebuild of ψ₇ over ℚ, sympy gcd, squarefreeness mod p — none of which enters Lean)
table.counterexample_if_dropped: n/a — no droppable hypotheses; the certificate either checks or does not.
prose_issues: Header title and docstring claim the point-level 'E[2] ⊥ E[7]' disjointness and the y ≠ 0 consequence, both strictly stronger than the polynomial-coprimality type

'No new axioms beyond the compiler trust of native_decide' is accurate but sits in tension with the repository's blanket 'Never add axioms' rule — native_decide extends the trusted base to the compiler

red_flags: Docstring overreach in CoprimePsi2Psi7.lean: the theorem type is IsCoprime secp256k1.Ψ₂Sq (secp256k1.preΨ' 7) over 𝔽_p[X], but the header sells it as 'E[2] ⊥ E[7]: no nonidentity point is simultaneously 2-torsion and 7-torsion' and 'transported to 𝔽̄_p it says y ≠ 0 at every root of preΨ₇'. Roots→points, y ≠ 0 (which needs Ψ₂Sq(x) = 4y² on the curve and char ≠ 2), and torsion-order reasoning are all unformalized at this layer.

Transitive native_decide trust is systematically understated: DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean advertises 'No native_decide in this file' while its headline theorem secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq depends on 5+5 native_decide calls in CoprimePsi2Psi3/CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 and further native_decide facts in Secp256k1Curve.lean; CoprimePsi2Psi7 adds 9 more. Every 'kernel is the only judge' claim in this stack actually includes the Lean compiler in the trusted base.

Sharpness of the Ward hypotheses (¬(b=0∧c=0), ¬(c=0∧d=0)) is claimed as 'sharp'/'both necessary' in docstrings but never formalized as Lean counterexample lemmas; the Python script executes only the (b,0,0) case and leaves (0,0,d) as a comment.

The design-insurance script scripts/certs/normeds_consecutive_zeros_check.py covers a strict subset of the theorem: single small prime p = 10007, seeded randomness, positive indices ≤ 320 only, and b is always sampled nonzero (make_get's even recurrence divides by b, and loop 3 draws b from range(1, p), making its degenerate-pair skip-guard partially dead code). The Lean theorem's b = 0, c ≠ 0 regime is numerically untested. Acceptable only because the script feeds nothing into the proofs — which the file states.

The script rebuilds W from the normEDS_odd/normEDS_even recurrences rather than Mathlib's normEDS definition (preNormEDS + parity twist), so it validates the recurrences' consequences, not the definition — a transcription-check gap the script itself flags as 'transcription/design insurance only'.

secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq at n = 0 is trivially true (Φ 0 = 1, IsCoprime 1 0), and at |n| = 1 both entries are units; 'for every n : ℤ' is honest but the non-vacuous content starts at |n| ≥ 2 — worth remembering when the result is cited as covering 'all n' in PR prose.

For p ∣ n the coprimality is true and kernel-proved but its torsion interpretation weakens: on an ordinary curve E[p] ≅ ℤ/p, so coprimality of Φ p / ΨSq p must NOT be read as a step toward #E[p] = p²; the docstring's parenthetical acknowledges the degree collapse but still frames the case as input to N10(i) degree bookkeeping.

The pasted Bézout constants in CoprimePsi2Psi7 (9 × 256-bit numbers) are claimed machine-emitted with 'zero hand transcription'; the claim is only as strong as the un-audited generator script — however, soundness does not depend on it: wrong constants would fail native_decide, so the risk is provenance/reproducibility, not correctness.
duplication_notes: CoprimePsi2Psi7.lean is an explicit architectural near-copy of CoprimePsi2Psi5.lean ('the nine-power sibling of the five-power CoprimePsi2Psi5', same private U/V constant naming — the docstring even warns the names shadow the same-named private constants of the sibling), and the family CoprimePsi2Psi3 / CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 / CoprimePsi3Psi5 / CoprimePsi3Psi7 / CoprimePsi2PrePsi4 all share the identical pattern: pasted constants → sparse Bézout product → one collapsing `key` ring identity → k native_decide residue equations. A single parametrized certificate-checker lemma (or a macro/elab) would replace ~6 files.

DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean repeats the transport idiom `have h := X.map (Polynomial.mapRingHom φbar); simpa only [Polynomial.coe_mapRingHom, secp256k1Bar, WeierstrassCurve.map_*] using h` three times (Ψ₂Sq/Ψ₃, Ψ₃/preΨ₄, Φ/ΨSq); a generic 'IsCoprime transports along secp256k1 → secp256k1Bar' helper would deduplicate and would also serve the announced N10(i) work.

The private `no_common_root` in DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean (coprime ⇒ no common eval root) duplicates reasoning already packaged in CoprimeCommonRoot.lean's L1 layer; it is 5 lines, but it is the third place in the stack where 'evaluate the Bézout identity' appears.

NormEDSConsecutiveZeros.lean's two public wrappers (pair (n, n+1) vs pair (n−1, n)) exist solely to match the disjunction shape emitted by DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge's descent — fine, but note the primed variant is pure reindexing.
audit.summary: The eds-coprimality layer checks out at the kernel level and the headline claims match the types: (1) NormEDSConsecutiveZeros.lean proves Ward apparition rigidity abstractly — over any integral domain (no field, no characteristic hypothesis), normEDS b c d has no two consecutive zeros given ¬(b=0∧c=0) and ¬(c=0∧d=0); the argument (minimal zero ρ ≥ 2, translation identity from the ported normEDS_isEllSequence, Case A divisibility descent / Case B small-ρ elimination) is fully formalized with no native_decide, and negative indices are handled by normEDS_neg reflection. (2) DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean composes this with the PR #171 evaluation bridge and the two n-independent Bézout certificates to get secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq (n : ℤ) : IsCoprime (secp256k1.Φ n) (secp256k1.ΨSq n) over 𝔽_p[X], plus the mapped 𝔽̄_p version. The quantifier over all n : ℤ is genuine — the certificates are n-independent — but n = 0 and |n| = 1 are trivial instances, and for p ∣ n the statement, while true and proved, must not be read as torsion-counting content (E[p] ≠ (ℤ/p)² on an ordinary curve); the theorem itself never claims points, counts, roots→points, y-coordinates, or squarefreeness. (3) CoprimePsi2Psi7.lean is a standard 9-residue native_decide Bézout certificate for IsCoprime Ψ₂Sq (preΨ' 7); its concrete polynomials match the previously proved secp256k1_Ψ₂Sq and secp256k1_preΨ₇ verbatim, but its docstring oversells the polynomial fact as point-level E[2] ⊥ E[7] disjointness and a y ≠ 0 statement that live downstream. Main systemic issues: transitive native_decide (compiler) trust is understated by 'no native_decide in this file' phrasing; hypothesis sharpness for the Ward theorem is prose-only; and the Python insurance script (p = 10007, seeded, b ≠ 0 always, indices ≤ 320, recurrence-based rather than definition-based) covers a strict subset of what the Lean theorems assert — harmless only because it feeds nothing into the proofs.

rows.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq
rows.correction: Signature, n-quantifier analysis, allowed_interpretation, and does_not_follow all verified accurate. Two corrections. (1) prose_issues claims the theorem 'transitively rests on 10+ native_decide residue checks in CoprimePsi2Psi3/CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 plus native_decide facts in Secp256k1Curve': the two certificate files contribute 3+4 = 7 residue checks, not 10+, and Secp256k1Curve.lean's two native_decide facts (secp256k1_Δ_ne_zero, generator coordinate check) are NOT in this theorem's dependency cone — no IsElliptic or generator fact is used anywhere in the coprimality chain (verified by grep over DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge/DivisionPolynomialCoprime/CoprimePsi2Psi3/CoprimePsi3PrePsi4/DivisionPolynomial/CoprimeCommonRoot). (2) The dominant transitive native_decide contributor is Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean — 71 native_decide occurrences in the Pratt primality certificate — which enters through the global instance `Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)` (Secp256k1PrimeP.lean:196, explicitly imported by DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean): the proof route needs Field (ZMod p) for AlgebraicClosure and for exists_common_root_of_not_isCoprime's [Field k] hypothesis (CoprimeCommonRoot.lean:28). The row's 'ZMod Secp256k1.p is a field via the concrete prime' understates this: the field structure itself rests on the largest native_decide surface in the whole stack. Also minor: exists_common_root_of_not_isCoprime is an indirect dependency (used inside the eval bridge at DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean:151), not called in this file.

rows.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_preΨ₇
rows.correction: Independently re-verified: exactly 9 native_decide residue equations (e24…e0), exactly 9 private constants (U₂₁…U₀, V₀), and both concrete polynomials in the `key` identity match secp256k1_Ψ₂Sq (DivisionPolynomial.lean:50, C 4 * X^3 + C 28) and secp256k1_preΨ₇ (CoprimePsi3Psi7.lean:46-50) coefficient-for-coefficient. The script claims also verified: sympy gcd over ℚ, recurrence rebuild of ψ₇ from ψ₀…ψ₄ with reduce_y mod y² − (x³+7), GF(p) Bézout + sparsity-on-X^{3k} assertions, ψ₇ squarefree mod p, prints CERT_OK, and emits the Lean file. Docstring overreach (point-level 'E[2] ⊥ E[7]', y ≠ 0 at roots of preΨ₇) confirmed as stated — strictly stronger than the IsCoprime type.

missed_issues: Secp256k1PrimeP.lean Pratt certificate is the dominant missed trust item: the Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p) global instance (Secp256k1PrimeP.lean:196), whose proof chain contains 71 native_decide occurrences, is in the dependency cone of secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq and both secp256k1Bar_* transports — DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean imports it explicitly, and the closure route (AlgebraicClosure needs Field (ZMod p); exists_common_root_of_not_isCoprime needs [Field k]) cannot elaborate without it. The audit's transitive-trust red flag both undercounts the certificate files (3+4 = 7, not '5+5'/'10+') and misattributes the remainder to Secp256k1Curve.lean, whose two native_decide facts (Δ ≠ 0, generator equation) are not used by this chain.

Red-flag error in the other direction: 'at |n| = 1 both entries are units' is false — Φ 1 = X is not a unit; the n = ±1 instance is trivial only because ΨSq 1 = 1 is a unit (IsCoprime f 1 always holds). The substantive point (non-vacuous content starts at |n| ≥ 2) survives, but the stated reason is wrong.

The audit did not note that psi2_psi7_coprime_cert.py also numerically checks gcd(ψ₇, x³ + 7) = 1 mod p — i.e. the y = 0 locus avoids every ψ₇ root, which is precisely the unformalized 'y ≠ 0' docstring claim the audit flags. The claim thus has Python-level numeric support (script section (c)), but remains unformalized in Lean; the audit's conclusion stands, the evidence inventory was incomplete.

NormEDSIsElliptic.lean's normEDS_isEllSequence (line 555) was cited but not characterized as a trust surface: it is a ~550-line third-party port ('adapting for 2024 → v4.31 API drift', per its own header 'not original work of this repository') of an unmerged mathlib4 PR (#13155) — the single largest unreviewed proof mass under the Ward theorem, though it is pure kernel-checked Lean with no native_decide, so the risk is provenance/maintenance, not soundness.
verify.overall: The audit table is substantially accurate and appropriately adversarial: all ten quoted signatures match the sources verbatim, the hypothesis inventories are complete (IsDomain, h23/h34, absence of characteristic hypotheses, arbitrary n : ℤ all correctly characterized), the does_not_follow lists correctly identify the roots→points, y ≠ 0, torsion-counting, and char-p-interpretation gaps, the evidence-class assignments are defensible, and every specific claim about the two Python scripts (p = 10007, seeded, indices < 320, b never sampled zero, (0,0,d) comment-only, recurrence-based rebuild; CERT_OK pipeline for the ψ₇ certificate) was verified true against the sources. The proof-structure descriptions of no_consec_aux (minimal zero via Nat.sInf, ρ ≥ 2, Case A divisibility descent, Case B small-ρ elimination via normEDS_odd, negative-index reflection via normEDS_neg) match the code exactly. Three rows needed correction, all on the same axis: the transitive native_decide accounting is wrong in detail — CoprimePsi2Psi3 has 3 (not 5) and CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 has 4 (not 5) native_decide calls, and the largest compiler-trust contributor in the cone of secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq is the missed Secp256k1PrimeP.lean Pratt primality certificate (71 native_decide occurrences, entering via the global Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance the closure route requires), while the flagged Secp256k1Curve.lean native_decide facts are not actually in the cone. One red flag overstates in the opposite direction ('at |n| = 1 both entries are units' — Φ 1 = X is not a unit). Net: the audit's qualitative thesis — kernel-sound theorems whose docstrings oversell point-level meaning, with systematically understated compiler trust — is confirmed and in fact strengthened by the corrected accounting.

table.name: Φ₃_eval (private)
table.exact_type: private theorem Φ₃_eval (x : ZMod Secp256k1.p) : (secp256k1.Φ 3).eval x = x ^ 9 - 672 * x ^ 6 + 2352 * x ^ 3 + 21952
hypotheses: none beyond x : ZMod Secp256k1.p (commutative-ring level; stated before the [Fact p.Prime] variable line)
table.allowed_interpretation: Mathlib's canonical Φ 3 for secp256k1, evaluated at any x in F_p, equals the explicit degree-9 numeral polynomial. This private lemma is the load-bearing link identifying the numerals used throughout the file with Mathlib's division polynomial (via secp256k1.Φ_three, secp256k1_Ψ₃_eval, secp256k1_preΨ₄_eval, secp256k1_Ψ₂Sq_eval).
does_not_follow: nothing about roots, torsion, or points
no degree or leading-coefficient claim
the integer literals are mod-p residues, not integer EDS values

table.name: ΨSq₃_eval (private)
table.exact_type: private theorem ΨSq₃_eval (x : ZMod Secp256k1.p) : (secp256k1.ΨSq 3).eval x = 9 * x ^ 8 + 504 * x ^ 5 + 7056 * x ^ 2
hypotheses: none beyond x : ZMod Secp256k1.p
table.allowed_interpretation: Mathlib's ΨSq 3 evaluated at x in F_p equals 9x⁸+504x⁵+7056x², which the file elsewhere rewrites as (3x⁴+84x)² = Ψ₃(x)². Identification lemma only.
does_not_follow: no nonvanishing claim (that is derived per-branch in the main theorem)
nothing about torsion

table.name: triple_x_core
hypotheses: [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)] (section variable; needed for Field (ZMod p) / division)
hcurve : y ^ 2 = x ^ 3 + 7
hy : y ≠ 0
h2 : (2 : ZMod Secp256k1.p) ≠ 0
hl2 : l2 * (2 * y) = 3 * x ^ 2 (cleared tangent slope)
hl3 : cleared chord-slope equation
table.allowed_interpretation: A pure field identity in F_p: given the cleared tangent- and chord-slope equations and the curve equation, the chord x-coordinate l3² − x(2P) − x equals the explicit degree-9/degree-8 fraction. The denominator's nonvanishing is derived inside the proof from hd (via (l2²−3x)·4y² = −Ψ₃(x)); the statement itself is total-division form. Certificates (hmaster etc.) are linear_combination steps elaborated and checked by the kernel.
does_not_follow: says nothing about elliptic-curve points or the group law — the caller must prove the slope equations correspond to 2P and 2P+P
does not mention Mathlib's Φ/ΨSq (explicit numerals only; identification is via the private eval lemmas)
does not export Ψ₃(x) ≠ 0 or ΨSq₃(x) ≠ 0

hy and hd are actually redundant given the other hypotheses (y=0 with hl2+hcurve forces 0=7; hd=0 with hl3+hy is contradictory) — over-hypothesized, which is sound but means the lemma is weaker-looking than needed
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hd (and hy): at x = 0 (so y² = 7) the denominator 9x⁸+504x⁵+7056x² is 0, RHS is Lean-junk 0, while LHS l3²−l2²−... is generically nonzero — the identity fails.
depends_on: Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p) instance (Secp256k1PrimeP.lean, Pratt certificate via native_decide, when instantiated)
prose_issues: docstring calls hmaster 'symbolically designed ... re-checked by the kernel' — accurate, but the sympy provenance script is not part of the trust chain and was not independently audited here

table.name: secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃
table.exact_type: theorem secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃ {x y X Y : ZMod Secp256k1.p} (h : secp256k1.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) {h' : secp256k1.toAffine.Nonsingular X Y} (hEq : (3 : ℕ) • (Point.some x y h) = Point.some X Y h') : X = (secp256k1.Φ 3).eval x / (secp256k1.ΨSq 3).eval x
hypotheses: [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)] (section variable)
h : P = (x,y) is an affine nonsingular F_p-point (so P ≠ O by construction)
h' : (X,Y) nonsingular (implicit binder)
hEq : (3 : ℕ) • P = Point.some X Y h' — this IS the side condition 3P ≠ O, encoded as 'the hypothesis is only satisfiable when 3P is affine'
table.allowed_interpretation: For every F_p-rational affine point P=(x,y) on secp256k1 with 3P also affine, x(3P) = Φ₃(x)/ΨSq₃(x) as ZMod-field division (Mathlib's Φ/ΨSq via the private eval lemmas). Two branches: generic (tangent-then-chord + triple_x_core) and y = negY (⟹ y = 0, 3P = P; formula holds because Ψ₂Sq(x)=0 collapses Φ₃ to x·ΨSq₃, with ΨSq₃(x) ≠ 0 from the Ψ₂Sq⊥Ψ₃ Bézout certificate). The proof establishes ΨSq₃(x) ≠ 0 in every reachable case, so the division is never junk under the hypotheses.
does_not_follow: nothing when 3P = O (hypothesis unsatisfiable) — the converse direction 'ΨSq₃(x)=0 ⟺ 3P=O' is NOT here (it lives in ThreeTorsionBridge, F_p only)
the conclusion does NOT export (ΨSq 3).eval x ≠ 0; a consumer wanting the cross-multiplied identity X·ΨSq₃(x) = Φ₃(x) must re-derive nonvanishing
no formula for Y (x-coordinate only)
F_p-rational points only — no statement over F_p^k or AlgebraicClosure, so it does not directly serve closure-side E[3] counting (secp256k1Bar track)
not the general-n formula: n=3 hard-coded numerals, no recursion
the 2-torsion branch is vacuous in the actual group (#E(F_p) = n is a 256-bit prime, so no F_p-point has y = 0) — real content over F_p is the generic branch only
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hEq (statement-form): for a 3-torsion point (over an extension) ΨSq₃(x)=0, RHS = Φ₃(x)/0 = 0 by Lean junk-division, and no affine 3P exists — X = 0 would be an unjustified assertion.
depends_on: Point.add_self_of_Y_eq / add_self_of_Y_ne / add_some, nonsingular_add, slope_of_Y_ne, slope_of_X_ne (Mathlib)
prose_issues: docstring/PR claim 'with no side conditions beyond 3 • P ≠ O' undersells: P ≠ O is also required (P is given as Point.some); 'no side conditions' means only that no extra nonvanishing hypotheses appear beyond what hEq encodes

docstring 'No native_decide, no new axioms' is true of this FILE only: the theorem transitively depends on Lean.ofReduceBool via the Ψ₂Sq⊥Ψ₃ Bézout certificate (used in the 2-torsion branch) and via the primality Fact instance when instantiated

table.name: secp256k1_preΨ₆ (private)
hypotheses: none (polynomial identity in (ZMod Secp256k1.p)[X])
table.allowed_interpretation: The 6th pre-division polynomial (odd part) of secp256k1 in explicit form, derived from Mathlib's recurrence preΨ'_even at m = 0 plus already-proved secp256k1_preΨ₅ (from CoprimePsi3Psi5.lean) — a genuinely new identity in this PR, needed for Φ 5 via Φ_ofNat.
does_not_follow: nothing about roots/6-torsion
coefficients are residues mod p of the integer EDS values

table.name: preΨ₅_eval (private)
table.exact_type: private theorem preΨ₅_eval (x : ZMod Secp256k1.p) : (secp256k1.preΨ' 5).eval x = 5 * x ^ 12 + 2660 * x ^ 9 - 11760 * x ^ 6 - 548800 * x ^ 3 - 614656
hypotheses: none beyond x : ZMod Secp256k1.p
table.allowed_interpretation: Thin evaluation wrapper over the existing secp256k1_preΨ₅ polynomial identity; used to plug Bézout evaluations into the torsion branches.
does_not_follow: no nonvanishing claim

table.name: Φ₅_eval (private)
hypotheses: none beyond x : ZMod Secp256k1.p
table.allowed_interpretation: Identifies Mathlib's Φ 5 (ℤ-indexed, unfolded via Φ_ofNat at n = 4: Φ 5 = X·preΨ'₅² − preΨ'₆·preΨ'₄·Ψ₂Sq) with the explicit degree-25 numeral polynomial over F_p. Load-bearing: without it the main theorem would be about ad-hoc numerals, not Mathlib's division polynomial.
does_not_follow: no degree/monicity claim
nothing about roots

table.name: ΨSq₅_eval (private)
hypotheses: none beyond x : ZMod Secp256k1.p
table.allowed_interpretation: Mathlib's ΨSq 5 = (preΨ'₅)² (5 odd, via ΨSq_ofNat) evaluated; the file elsewhere re-derives the square shape inline to get nonvanishing from preΨ₅(x) ≠ 0.
does_not_follow: no nonvanishing claim

table.name: quint_x_core
hypotheses: [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)] (section variable)
hcurve, hy, h2 as in triple_x_core
hl2 : cleared tangent slope
hd : x(2P) − x ≠ 0
hl3 : cleared chord slope 2P→P
hd5 : x(3P) − x(2P) ≠ 0 (= s3² − 2s2² + 3x, supplied by caller)
hl5 : cleared chord slope 3P→2P
table.allowed_interpretation: Pure field identity: under the three cleared slope equations and two chord-denominator nonvanishings, the second-chord x-coordinate equals the explicit Φ₅/ΨSq₅ fraction. Internally hquint shows (x(3P)−x(2P))·(x(2P)−x)²·64y⁶ = −preΨ₅(x), from which the ΨSq₅ denominator nonvanishing follows given hd5; the ~90-line hmaster cofactors are opaque but kernel-checked linear_combination steps.
does_not_follow: no point-level content: the caller must connect s2/s3/s5 to actual group-law slopes and must prove hd5 (in the main theorem this comes from 5P ≠ O via five_core — that implication is NOT in this lemma)
explicit numerals only; Mathlib Φ/ΨSq identification is external (private eval lemmas)
does not export preΨ₅(x) ≠ 0 or ΨSq₅(x) ≠ 0
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hd5: for P a 5-torsion configuration (statement-form), preΨ₅(x)=0 makes the denominator 0, RHS = junk 0, while the LHS chord expression need not be 0.

table.name: secp256k1_quintuple_x_eq_Φ₅_div_ΨSq₅
table.exact_type: theorem secp256k1_quintuple_x_eq_Φ₅_div_ΨSq₅ {x y X Y : ZMod Secp256k1.p} (h : secp256k1.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) {h' : secp256k1.toAffine.Nonsingular X Y} (hEq : (5 : ℕ) • (Point.some x y h) = Point.some X Y h') : X = (secp256k1.Φ 5).eval x / (secp256k1.ΨSq 5).eval x
hypotheses: [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)] (section variable)
h : P affine nonsingular F_p-point (P ≠ O by construction)
h' implicit
hEq : (5 : ℕ) • P = Point.some X Y h' — encodes 5P ≠ O
table.allowed_interpretation: For every F_p-rational affine P with 5P affine, x(5P) = Φ₅(x)/ΨSq₅(x) as ZMod-field division. Three exhaustive branches: (a) y = negY (⟹ y=0, 5P = P; ΨSq₅(x) ≠ 0 from Ψ₂Sq⊥preΨ₅ Bézout); (b) 3P = O (5P = 2P, reduces to the n=2 formula, cross-multiplied via div_eq_div_iff with the difference divisible by Ψ₃; ΨSq₅(x) ≠ 0 from Ψ₃⊥preΨ₅ Bézout); (c) generic 5P = 3P+2P (tangent-chord-chord; the chord denominator x(3P) ≠ x(2P) is derived from 5P ≠ O via five_core + secp256k1_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff, then quint_x_core closes). Denominator nonvanishing is established internally in all three branches.
does_not_follow: nothing when 5P = O; the converse 'ΨSq₅(x)=0 ⟺ 5P=O' is FiveTorsionBridge (F_p only), not this theorem
(ΨSq 5).eval x ≠ 0 not exported in the conclusion (total-division statement)
x-coordinate only, no Y formula
F_p-rational points only — not the closure, so unusable directly for #E[5] = 25 counting (E[5] ⊄ E(F_p); in fact E(F_p) has prime order n so its only 5-torsion point is O)
branches (a) and (b) are vacuous in the actual group (no F_p-point has y=0 or 3P=O since #E(F_p) is a 256-bit prime coprime to 30) — the genuinely inhabited case over F_p is the generic branch
n=5 hard-coded; no general-n statement or induction
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hEq: for a 5-torsion x (over an extension) preΨ₅(x)=0, denominator = 0, RHS = junk 0 with no affine 5P — the equality would be an unfounded claim.
prose_issues: 'no side conditions beyond 5 • P ≠ O' — P ≠ O is also required (P is Point.some); 'no side conditions' means the hypothesis hEq itself encodes the classical exclusion

docstring honestly discloses the imported Bézout native_decide residue checks, but neither file mentions that the Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance (needed for the field structure) is also native_decide-backed (Pratt certificate, Secp256k1PrimeP.lean)

red_flags: 'Side-condition-free' PR/docstring framing is marketing: both main theorems carry the classical side condition n•P ≠ O (and P ≠ O) inside the hypothesis hEq : (n : ℕ) • Point.some x y h = Point.some X Y h'. At points where ΨSq n vanishes (n-torsion), hEq is unsatisfiable and the theorems say nothing; at O there is no statement at all. What IS true: no extra nonvanishing hypotheses beyond hEq, and the y=0 (2-torsion) and 3P=O cases are genuinely proved rather than excluded.

TripleMultiplicationFormula.lean docstring claims 'No native_decide, no new axioms' — true only file-locally. secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃'s 2-torsion branch uses secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃, whose proof is native_decide-based (CoprimePsi2Psi3.lean:37-39), and the Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p) instance it needs for field division is a Pratt certificate riddled with native_decide (Secp256k1PrimeP.lean). The theorem's axiom footprint therefore includes Lean.ofReduceBool (compiler trust). The quintuple file discloses the Bézout residue but neither file discloses the primality-instance dependence.

The conclusions are equalities of field elements under ZMod's total (junk-value) division: X = Φₙ.eval x / ΨSqₙ.eval x. The denominators' nonvanishing is proved internally in every branch but NOT exported as a conjunct, so a consumer wanting the cross-multiplied identity X · ΨSqₙ(x) = Φₙ(x) must re-derive ΨSqₙ(x) ≠ 0 from the bridges/Bézout lemmas.

Both theorems are over F_p (ZMod Secp256k1.p) only, NOT AlgebraicClosure. Since #E(F_p) = n is a 256-bit prime coprime to 2·3·5, the 2-torsion branch (both files) and the 3-torsion branch (quintuple) are vacuous over F_p — no rational point triggers them — and the theorems cannot serve closure-side E[3]/E[5] point counting (the secp256k1Bar track) without being re-proved over the closure. The branch handling is honest robustness, not new torsion content.
Audit performed by source reading only (no lake build); both files are imported by Ecdlp.lean at SHA 5f61fa5, so the kernel_theorem classification is contingent on PR #172 CI being green.

The linear_combination cofactors, especially the ~90-line hmaster in quint_x_core, are humanly unreviewable; soundness rests entirely on kernel elaboration + ring normalization. The provenance scripts (scripts/certs/triple_mult_formula_check.py, quint_mult_formula_check.py) are correctly outside the trust chain but were not independently audited.
duplication_notes: The generic-branch tangent-then-chord scaffolding (~55 lines: h2/hcurve/hnegY extraction, hsl2, hId, hd, hx2val/hx2x/hx2ne, hy2val, hns2/hP2, s3/X3/Y3, hx3val, hns3, hP3, hsl3s, hl3) is duplicated near-verbatim between TripleMultiplicationFormula.lean and QuintupleMultiplicationFormula.lean, and per the docstrings mirrors FiveTorsionBridge.lean a third time. A shared 'tangent-chord setup' lemma (returning hP3, hl3, hd as a bundle) would collapse all three.

The 2-torsion branch is a copy-paste pattern in both files: nP-collapse via add_self_of_Y_eq, hy00 (y=0 from char ≠ 2), hΨ2z (4x³+28 = 0), Bézout-eval contradiction for denominator ≠ 0, denominator-as-square rewrite, eq_div_iff, closing linear_combination — only the Bézout certificate and cofactor differ.
The doubling identities hId ((s2²−3x)·4y² = −Ψ₃(x)) and hIdsq are restated four times across the two files (both cores, both main-theorem generic branches).

The 'ΨSqₙ(x) = (preΨₙ(x))² hence ≠ 0' inline rw [show ...] block appears three times inside QuintupleMultiplicationFormula.lean alone (quint_x_core, 2-torsion branch, 3-torsion branch) and once in the triple file — one private lemma per file would remove ~40 lines.

h2 : (2 : ZMod p) ≠ 0 and the hcurve extraction from Nonsingular are re-proved inline in both main theorems despite equivalents existing in imported files (e.g. ThreeTorsionBridge proves the same h2 the same way).
audit.summary: Both N7 rungs are real, fully-proved kernel theorems over F_p = ZMod Secp256k1.p: for an affine F_p-point P with n•P affine (n = 3, 5), x(nP) = (Φ n).eval x / (ΨSq n).eval x as Lean field division, with the Mathlib-Φ/ΨSq identification carried by private eval lemmas and denominator nonvanishing proved internally (via Bézout coprimality certificates and the ψ₃/ψ₅ torsion bridges) but not exported. The 'no side conditions' claim is accurate only in the narrow sense that hEq itself encodes nP ≠ O and P ≠ O; the statements are silent at O and at n-torsion points, are F_p-only (not the closure, where E[3]/E[5] actually live), and the explicitly handled 2-/3-torsion branches are vacuous in the real prime-order group. The triple file's 'No native_decide, no new axioms' docstring is misleading at theorem granularity — both theorems transitively depend on Lean.ofReduceBool through the native_decide-based Bézout certificates and the Pratt-certificate primality instance. Substantial near-verbatim duplication of the tangent-chord scaffolding, 2-torsion branch, and nonvanishing boilerplate exists between the two files and FiveTorsionBridge.

rows.name: triple_x_core
rows.correction: Everything except counterexample_if_dropped is accurate (signature verbatim; hypotheses complete; the redundancy observation for hy and hd is correct). But counterexample_if_dropped contradicts the row's own redundancy note and is invalid: dropping hd (and hy) does NOT make the identity fail. At the proposed witness x = 0, the retained hypothesis hl3 becomes (l2²−0)·l3 = −2y with hl2 forcing l2 = 0, so 0 = −2y, hence y = 0 (h2), and then hl2+hcurve force 3x² = 0 ⟹ x = 0 ⟹ 7 = 0 in ZMod p — contradiction. The hypothesis set minus {hd, hy} is unsatisfiable in every such configuration, so the theorem with hd and hy dropped remains TRUE (they are derivable, as the row itself says); dropping them only breaks this particular proof script, it does not admit a counterexample. Correct framing: hd/hy are proof-scaffolding hypotheses whose removal changes nothing mathematically.

rows.name: secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃
rows.correction: All claims verified against source: signature verbatim; 2-torsion branch uses secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃ whose proof is native_decide (CoprimePsi2Psi3.lean:37-39 confirmed), so the theorem's axiom footprint includes Lean.ofReduceBool despite the file docstring's 'No native_decide, no new axioms'; generic branch derives ΨSq₃(x) ≠ 0 from 3P ≠ O via secp256k1_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff (ThreeTorsionBridge.lean:38, native_decide-free — verified). One clarification the row already half-makes: the primality Fact is a section-variable hypothesis, so Secp256k1PrimeP's native_decide enters only at instantiation, and the row says 'when instantiated' — accurate.

rows.name: Φ₅_eval (private)
rows.correction: Row accurate; trivial omission only: the proof also rewrites with Mathlib's WeierstrassCurve.preΨ'_four, not listed in depends_on. Immaterial.

rows.name: quint_x_core
rows.correction: Everything except counterexample_if_dropped is accurate (signature verbatim modulo the flagged '...' elision — elided numerals match Φ₅_eval/ΨSq₅_eval exactly; hquint at line 156 and the ~84-line hmaster verified). But the counterexample is not a counterexample: with hl5 retained, s3²−2s2²+3x = 0 forces 0 = 2·(s2(s2²−3x)+y) (h2), i.e. the y-coordinate of 2P vanishes; hcurve+hl2 make (X2,Y2) a curve point by polynomial identity, so X2³+7 = 0 would need an F_p-root of x³+7 — impossible for secp256k1 (#E(F_p) = n is odd, so E(F_p) has no 2-torsion, equivalently −7 is not a cube mod p). Hence the hypothesis set minus hd5 is unsatisfiable in every preΨ₅(x) = 0 configuration and the statement with hd5 dropped remains true (vacuously there); 'the LHS chord expression need not be 0' is never realized. Unlike hd in triple_x_core, hd5's redundancy rests on a deep global fact not available to the file's certificate, so keeping hd5 is the right design — but the claimed failure mode is wrong.

rows.name: secp256k1_quintuple_x_eq_Φ₅_div_ΨSq₅

red_flags: No `Squarefree` statement exists in DivisionPolynomialSquarefree.lean despite the file name: all three theorems are `IsCoprime f (derivative f)`. Mathematically sound (IsCoprime is the stronger, right notion), but every per-theorem docstring headlines 'is squarefree, by certificate' — vocabulary the kernel never checked. The module docstring admits the deferral; the theorem docstrings do not.

The advertised deliverable 'exactly (n²−1)/2 DISTINCT roots over 𝔽̄_p' is never a single theorem. It is split into Multiset.card-with-multiplicity (= 4/12/24) plus a separate Nodup; no toFinset.card statement packages them. Downstream consumers must re-derive the conjunction.

Card-with-multiplicity = natDegree holds for ANY polynomial over the closure. The naming/docstring ('a separable polynomial ... has exactly that many roots') misattributes the count to B4 and invites reading card_bar as a distinct-root count.

All six headline results ultimately rest on `native_decide` (Lean.ofReduceBool compiler trust): directly for the 3 IsCoprime certificates (3+8+16 residue equations), inherited by all Separable/root-count theorems. The Separable file's 'no native_decide here' is literally true but does not shrink the trust base. Independent re-verification: I recomputed all three Bézout identities u·f + v·f' = 1 mod p and both preΨ' concrete forms against the standard ψ₅/ψ₇ recurrence for y² = x³ + 7 in Python — all pass, so the certificates are genuine.

Point-level claims are docstring-only in both files: no roots→points transition, no y-existence over 𝔽̄_p, no y ≠ 0 handling, no (x,y) vs (x,−y) distinctness, no counting of O, and no #E[n] = n² anywhere in this group. The Separable file's docstring is honest ('once the point-level bridge is transported to the closure — the next rung'); the Squarefree file's theorem docstrings assert '3-torsion x-locus has exactly 4 distinct points' unconditionally.

Characteristic hypotheses (char ≠ 2, char ≠ ℓ) are nowhere stated; they are implicitly discharged because everything is concrete over the fixed secp256k1 p (the certificate equations themselves exhibit units like 84, −614656, 377801998336 mod p). Nothing generalizes: B4 for n ∉ {3,5,7} or for any other curve/prime is open, as the module docstring concedes.

Unformalized prose claim in the Squarefree module docstring: coprimality-with-derivative 'is equivalent to' squarefreeness here — neither direction is formalized in the repo; only prose about char p being large.

`φac` is a PRIVATE abbrev occurring in the six public root-count theorem statements: downstream files cannot write the name and must re-spell `algebraMap (ZMod Secp256k1.p) (AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))` (reducible-defeq, so usable, but an API wart that encourages copy-paste).

Root counts are over AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p) only — nothing is claimed over F_p itself, which is correct and necessary (E(F_p) has prime order n, so rational ℓ-torsion for ℓ ∈ {3,5,7} is trivial; the full x-locus exists only over the closure).

A single generic 'sparse Bézout validator' lemma (coefficients as a function ℕ → ZMod p, residue classes mod 3) would replace ~450 lines of per-n boilerplate and shrink the native_decide surface to one decidable check per n.

The six public root-count theorems in DivisionPolynomialSeparable.lean are three mechanical instantiations of two PRIVATE generic lemmas (roots_card_map_of_separable / roots_nodup_map_of_separable). audit.summary: B4@{3,5,7} audit. DivisionPolynomialSquarefree.lean proves exactly three theorems, all of type IsCoprime f (derivative f) over F_p = ZMod Secp256k1.p for f = secp256k1.Ψ₃, secp256k1.preΨ' 5, secp256k1.preΨ' 7, each via an explicit Bézout witness whose residue equations are discharged by native_decide (ofReduceBool trust); I independently re-verified all three identities and both preΨ' concrete forms (against the standard ψ₅/ψ₇ recurrence for y²=x³+7) in Python — all genuine. Despite the file name, no Mathlib `Squarefree` statement exists; the question 'does Squarefree over F_p imply the closure root count' is moot because the chain never uses Squarefree: DivisionPolynomialSeparable.lean re-labels the certificates as Polynomial.Separable (definitional: Separable = IsCoprime f f'), then PROVES the transport to the closure formally — Separable.map + nodup_roots gives distinctness over AlgebraicClosure (ZMod p), and IsAlgClosed.splits + splits_iff_card_roots + natDegree_map_eq_of_injective gives Multiset.card roots = natDegree (4/12/24, imported degree lemmas from main). Two gaps to hold downstream consumers to: (a) the exact counts are Multiset.card WITH multiplicity plus a separate Nodup — 'exactly d distinct roots' (toFinset.card = d) is never stated as one theorem; (b) the card theorems do not use separability at all (the private helper's hf is unused), so citing _roots_card_bar alone as the B4 payoff is wrong — only the _nodup_bar theorems carry the certificate's content. Everything is x-locus only: no roots→points bridge, no y≠0, no ±y pairing, no O, no #E[n]=n² in this group; char hypotheses are implicit in the concrete p; scope is exactly n ∈ {3,5,7}.

rows.correction: Minor fix in allowed_interpretation: the standard recurrence is ψ₇ = ψ₅ψ₃³ − ψ₂ψ₄³ (in Mathlib normalization preΨ'7 = preΨ'5·Ψ₃³ − Ψ₂Sq²·preΨ₄³, since Ψ₂Sq = ψ₂² and preΨ₄ = ψ₄/ψ₂), not 'ψ₂²ψ₄³' as written. The substantive claim survives: I independently re-derived ψ₇ from the recurrence over ℤ and it matches the repo's degree-24 concrete form exactly, and the 16-equation Bézout identity re-verifies mod p. Everything else in the row (verbatim type, 8+8 cofactors on exponent classes ≡0/≡1 mod 3, 16 native_decide, CoprimePsi3Psi7.lean:46 dependency, does_not_follow list) is accurate.

rows.name: roots_card_map_of_separable (private, carries the counting content)
rows.correction: Row core is accurate and important (verbatim type; hf : f.Separable genuinely unused — proof is splits_iff_card_roots + IsAlgClosed.splits + natDegree_map_eq_of_injective; Fact instance at Secp256k1PrimeP.lean:195 confirmed). But the counterexample_if_dropped illustration is factually wrong for the concrete curve: −28 IS a cubic residue mod the secp256k1 p (p ≡ 1 mod 3, j = 0), and Ψ₃ = 3X(X³+28) has ALL 4 roots in F_p — verified by computing deg gcd(Ψ₃, X^p − X) = 4. So '= 4 can fail over the base field' is false for Ψ₃ specifically. The generic point stands (base-field roots.card can undershoot natDegree), and the closure IS genuinely essential for the other two: preΨ'5 and preΨ'7 have exactly 0 roots in F_p (deg gcd = 0, computed). A better dropped-hypothesis illustration is n = 5 or 7, not n = 3.

rows.name: secp256k1_preΨ₅_roots_card_bar
rows.correction: The counterexample_if_dropped parenthetical uses an invalid inference: 'E(F_p) has prime order n, so no rational 5-torsion' is true, but that does NOT bound F_p-roots of ψ₅ — an x-root in F_p can carry y ∈ F_p² \ F_p (a point on the quadratic twist). Concrete disproof of the inference pattern: ψ₃ has all 4 roots in F_p even though E(F_p) has no 3-torsion. The conclusion nevertheless holds, and more strongly than claimed: preΨ'5 has exactly 0 roots in F_p (verified via deg gcd(preΨ'5, X^p − X) = 0), so the closure is genuinely essential for the count 12. Also 'Same as Ψ₃ row' imports the wrong Ψ₃ illustration (see roots_card_map_of_separable correction). All formal content of the row (verbatim type, deps incl. FiveTorsion.lean:30, does_not_follow) is accurate.

missed_issues: Red flag 9's parenthetical 'the full x-locus exists only over the closure' is FALSE for n = 3: ψ₃ = 3X(X³+28) splits completely over F_p (all 4 roots rational; −28 is a cube mod p, consistent with j = 0 CM and p ≡ 1 mod 3; verified by deg gcd(ψ₃, X^p − X) = 4 at the concrete p). The audit systematically misses the rational-x-locus vs rational-points distinction (twist points): only the POINTS need the extension, and only for n = 5, 7 is the closure needed for the x-count itself (preΨ'5 and preΨ'7 have 0 roots in F_p, verified).

Trust-flow imprecision in red flag 4: the _roots_card_bar theorems inherit native_decide trust NOT through the B4 certificates (separability is unused in the card path) but through the natDegree lemmas on main — secp256k1_Ψ₃_natDegree (DivisionPolynomialDegree.lean:50) discharges 3 ≠ 0 via native_decide, and secp256k1_preΨ₅_natDegree / secp256k1_preΨ₇_natDegree (FiveTorsion.lean:30 / SevenTorsion.lean:29) discharge p ∤ 5 / p ∤ 7 via native_decide. So even the with-multiplicity counts are not native_decide-free; the audit attributes all inherited compiler trust to the certificates only.

PR #174 (this session's own PR, head 810de34) was never checked for overlap: it does not touch either audited file (no collision — verified via git diff --name-status against ac1ec94), but it adds Ecdlp/Proved/ElevenTorsion.lean and ThirteenTorsion.lean degree rungs, which makes the audit's duplication warning (private, ZMod Secp256k1.p-hardcoded generic lemmas roots_card_map_of_separable / roots_nodup_map_of_separable forcing re-copies at the next torsion prime) immediately actionable rather than hypothetical.

The Squarefree module docstring's provenance claim (cofactors from scripts/certs/psi_squarefree_certs.py, 'prints CERT_OK') was never checked by the auditor; the script does exist and is added in the same PR #172 — provenance is at least in-repo, a point in the PR's favor the audit omitted.

native_decide invocation count never stated precisely per file as a checkable figure: 27 tactic invocations in DivisionPolynomialSquarefree.lean (3 + 8 + 16, matching the per-theorem counts the audit gives), 0 in DivisionPolynomialSeparable.lean — confirmed by grep at the PR head.
verify.overall: High-quality audit: 11/14 rows CONFIRMED, 3 CORRECTED on peripheral fields, none REFUTED. Every quoted exact_type is verbatim-accurate against git show 5f61fa5 of both files; all seven dependency file:line citations on main (ac1ec94) check out exactly; evidence classes (certificate for the three IsCoprime theorems given ofReduceBool trust, derived_corollary elsewhere) are defensible; field-of-statement assignments (F_p for the certificates/Separable, AlgebraicClosure F_p for all root counts) are correct. The audit's three central findings all independently verify: (1) no Mathlib Squarefree statement exists despite the file name (grep: token appears only in docstrings); (2) 'exactly d distinct roots' is never packaged as one theorem — Multiset.card-with-multiplicity and Nodup are separate, no toFinset.card statement exists; (3) hf : f.Separable is genuinely unused in roots_card_map_of_separable, so only the _nodup_bar theorems carry the B4 certificate's content. I independently reproduced the auditor's claimed Python re-verification: all three Bézout identities u·f + v·f′ = 1 hold mod p with the file's constants, and the concrete preΨ'5/preΨ'7 forms match the standard division-polynomial recurrence for y² = x³ + 7 recomputed exactly over ℤ. The corrections are real but peripheral: a miswritten recurrence citation (ψ₂²ψ₄³ for ψ₂ψ₄³), and a recurring factual error in the counterexample narratives and red flag 9 — ψ₃ actually splits completely over F_p (4 rational roots, verified computationally), so the closure is essential for the x-count only at n = 5, 7 (0 roots in F_p each, verified), and 'no rational torsion ⇒ few F_p roots of ψₙ' is an invalid inference (twist points). One systematic trust-flow miss: the card theorems inherit native_decide through the natDegree lemmas on main, not through the certificates. Cross-PR claims verified: #173 touches only TripleDivisionPolynomial.lean among Proved; both audited files are new in #172 and imported in Ecdlp.lean (lines 120, 122) at the PR head; #174 (unchecked by the auditor) has no collision but adds the n = 11, 13 rungs that sharpen the duplication warning about the private, p-hardcoded helper lemmas.

table.name: secp256k1Bar_psi3_evalEval
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_psi3_evalEval (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p)) :
    (secp256k1Bar.ψ 3).evalEval x y = 3 * x ^ 4 + 84 * x
hypotheses: x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p) — arbitrary, NOT required to be on the curve
ambient instance Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p) (Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean) making ZMod p a field so AlgebraicClosure elaborates
table.allowed_interpretation: The Mathlib bivariate division polynomial ψ 3 ∈ K[X][Y] of the base-changed curve secp256k1Bar := secp256k1.map (algebraMap 𝔽_p 𝔽̄_p), evaluated via evalEval at ANY pair (x,y) ∈ 𝔽̄_p², equals the univariate value 3x⁴+84x. Valid off-curve because ψ 3 = C Ψ₃ is y-free (WeierstrassCurve.ψ_three). Proof route: Ψ₃ closed form transported by Polynomial.map along φK from the 𝔽_p closed form secp256k1_Ψ₃ (numeral coefficients fixed by the hom).
does_not_follow: Nothing about torsion or the group law
No root count of Ψ₃ (that lives in DivisionPolynomialSeparable.lean)
Does not identify ψ 3 with a factor of any multiplication-by-3 formula
table.counterexample_if_dropped: n/a — unconditional identity; the only latent hypothesis is injectivity of the base-change hom, used to keep coefficients as numerals (automatic).

table.name: secp256k1Bar_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
    (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (h : secp256k1Bar.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) :
    (3 : ℕ) • (Point.some x y h) = 0 ↔ (secp256k1Bar.ψ 3).evalEval x y = 0
hypotheses: h : secp256k1Bar.toAffine.Nonsingular x y (point is on the curve and nonsingular)
char facts are concrete, not abstract: p ∤ 2, 7, 63 proved by decide over ℕ in 𝔽_p, transported to 𝔽̄_p via injectivity of φK (φK_ne_zero)
table.allowed_interpretation: For every AFFINE point P = Point.some x y h of the closure curve — including y = 0 (2-torsion) points, since every nonzero point of the Point inductive is affine — 3•P = 0 in the Mathlib point group iff ψ₃ vanishes at (x,y). The y = negY branch is genuinely handled: there both sides are proved false (3•P = -2•P... shown ≠ 0 via Point.some_ne_zero, and 3x⁴+84x ≠ 0 forced from x³ = −7 and 63 ≠ 0). This is an independent re-proof over 𝔽̄_p (group-law case split, slope identity ℓ·2y = 3x², rational identity addX(P,P)−x = −(3x⁴+84x)/(4y²)); it is NOT transported from the 𝔽_p theorem secp256k1_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff, which it never references — only polynomial closed forms and constant-nonzero facts cross the base change.
does_not_follow: No statement about P = O (the iff is only stated for Point.some; 3•O = 0 is trivial but outside the statement)
Roots→points is NOT here: it does not say any root of Ψ₃ is the x-coordinate of an actual curve point
No count #E[3](𝔽̄_p) = 9, no (x,y)/(x,−y) distinctness, no E[3] ≅ ℤ/3 × ℤ/3 — all delegated to ThreeTorsionStructure.lean / TorsionStructure.lean
Not phrased via AddSubgroup.torsionBy; the translation nsmul → torsionBy membership is elsewhere
depends_on: Mathlib: Point.add_self_of_Y_eq, Point.add_of_Y_ne, slope_of_Y_ne, Point.neg_some, Point.some_ne_zero
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop h (Nonsingular): Point.some x y h is not well-formed; drop the 63 ≠ 0 transport: in a field where 63 = 0 (char 3 or 7) the y = 0 branch's RHS-false claim fails — e.g. char 3 kills 3x⁴+84x identically, making the RHS true for every point while 3•P = P ≠ 0 for a 2-torsion point.
prose_issues: Docstring: 'over 𝔽̄_p the four roots of Ψ₃ are realized by actual curve points, giving #E[3](𝔽̄_p) = 9 once paired with ±y' — realization, pairing distinctness, and the count 9 are NOT proved by this theorem or this file
Module docstring claims E[3](𝔽_p) = {O} — proved elsewhere in the repo, not here

table.name: secp256k1Bar_psi2_evalEval
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_psi2_evalEval (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p)) :
    secp256k1Bar.ψ₂.evalEval x y = 2 * y
hypotheses: none; (x,y) arbitrary, on-curve not required (ψ₂ = 2y + a₁x + a₃ with a₁ = a₃ = 0 after base change)
table.allowed_interpretation: The bivariate ψ₂ of secp256k1Bar evaluates to 2y at any (x,y). This is the y-carrying factor: for even-index division polynomials the (x,y)-form matters, and this lemma is what lets ψ 5 and ψ 7 (odd) collapse to x-only forms on-curve.
does_not_follow: Not the 2-torsion criterion (that is secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff below)

table.name: secp256k1Bar_psi5_evalEval
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_psi5_evalEval (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (hcurve : y ^ 2 = x ^ 3 + 7) :
    (secp256k1Bar.ψ 5).evalEval x y
      = 5 * x ^ 12 + 2660 * x ^ 9 - 11760 * x ^ 6 - 548800 * x ^ 3 - 614656
hypotheses: hcurve : y ^ 2 = x ^ 3 + 7 — ON-CURVE ONLY, unlike the n = 3 evaluation which is unconditional
table.allowed_interpretation: On the curve, the bivariate ψ 5 (via the Mathlib recurrence ψ_odd 2: ψ5 = ψ4·ψ2³ − ψ1·ψ3³) equals the stated degree-12 x-only polynomial; even powers of y are eliminated through hcurve by an explicit linear_combination certificate. The ψ-object evaluated is the bivariate ψ 5 ∈ K[X][Y] at (x,y); the x-only form is a derived on-curve normal form.
does_not_follow: FALSE off-curve: this is not a polynomial identity in K[X][Y]
No torsion content by itself
depends_on: Mathlib: WeierstrassCurve.ψ_odd, ψ_four, ψ_two, ψ_one, ψ_three
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hcurve: at y = 0 with x³ + 7 ≠ 0, ψ2 evalEval = 0 so (ψ 5).evalEval x 0 = −(3x⁴+84x)³, whose leading coefficient −27 differs from 5; the two degree-12 polynomials differ, so the identity fails at all but finitely many off-curve x.

table.name: five_core_bar
table.exact_type:
theorem five_core_bar (x y ℓ₂ ℓ₃ : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (h64 : (64 : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p)) ≠ 0)
    (hcurve : y ^ 2 = x ^ 3 + 7)
    (hy : y ≠ 0)
    (hℓ2 : 2 * y * ℓ₂ = 3 * x ^ 2)
    (hd : ℓ₂ ^ 2 - 3 * x ≠ 0)
    (hℓ3 : (ℓ₂ ^ 2 - 3 * x) * ℓ₃ = -(ℓ₂ * (ℓ₂ ^ 2 - 3 * x) + y) - y) :
    ℓ₃ ^ 2 - (ℓ₂ ^ 2 - 2 * x) - x = ℓ₂ ^ 2 - 2 * x
      ↔ 5 * x ^ 12 + 2660 * x ^ 9 - 11760 * x ^ 6 - 548800 * x ^ 3 - 614656 = 0
hypotheses: h64 : 64 ≠ 0 (char ≠ 2 in concrete form, passed in by caller from decide+transport)
hcurve, hy : y ≠ 0
hℓ2 (tangent-slope relation), hd (x(2P) ≠ x, i.e. the doubling secant denominator is nonzero), hℓ3 (secant-slope relation for 2P + P)
table.allowed_interpretation: PUBLIC but purely algebraic: an identity among field elements x, y, ℓ₂, ℓ₃ satisfying the listed relations — the type contains no curve points, no group law, no slopes-as-defined-objects. Its geometric reading (x(3P) = x(2P) ⟺ ψ₅(x) = 0) is established only by the caller secp256k1Bar_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff, which instantiates ℓ₂, ℓ₃ with actual Mathlib slopes. Proof is the 𝔽_p template's sympy-designed linear_combination master certificate re-elaborated verbatim over 𝔽̄_p (integer-numeral coefficients, valid over any commutative ring): an independent kernel re-proof, not a transport.
does_not_follow: No claim that such ℓ₂, ℓ₃ exist for a given point
The iff says nothing when ℓ₂² − 3x = 0 (the 3-torsion x-locus), which the caller must and does handle separately
depends_on: nothing curve-specific beyond field axioms; caller supplies all relations
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hd: pick ℓ₂ with ℓ₂² = 3x; then hℓ3 becomes 0 = −(ℓ₂·0+y)−y = −2y ≠ 0 is required... but with hℓ3 vacated ℓ₃ is unconstrained, and ℓ₃² − (ℓ₂²−2x) − x = ℓ₂²−2x can be toggled by choosing ℓ₃, independently of whether ψ₅(x) = 0. Drop hy/h64: (2y)⁶-clearing and 64y⁶ ≠ 0 in the certificate collapse.
prose_issues: Docstring speaks of 'the x-coordinate of 3P equals that of 2P' — stronger geometric language than the point-free algebraic type

table.name: secp256k1Bar_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
    (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (h : secp256k1Bar.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) :
    (5 : ℕ) • (Point.some x y h) = 0 ↔ (secp256k1Bar.ψ 5).evalEval x y = 0
hypotheses: h : Nonsingular x y
concrete char facts: 2, 3, 64, 1750329, 614656, 49787136 ≠ 0 in 𝔽̄_p, each from a decide over ℕ transported along injective φK — no abstract char ≠ 2/3/5 hypotheses
table.allowed_interpretation: For every affine point of the closure curve, 5•P = 0 iff the bivariate ψ 5 vanishes at (x,y) (equivalently, by the Stage-1 lemma, iff the degree-12 x-polynomial vanishes). Full case analysis: (i) y = 0 branch — both sides false (5P = P ≠ 0; on x³ = −7 the polynomial evaluates to the unit 1750329 = 3⁶·7⁴); (ii) 3-torsion branch (3•P = 0, routed through the ALREADY-PORTED closure bridge secp256k1Bar_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff, not the 𝔽_p one) — both sides false (5P = 2P ≠ 0 since y ≠ 0; polynomial is a unit on x = 0 and on x³ = −28); (iii) main branch via 5•P = 0 ⟺ 2P = −3P ⟺ x(2P) = x(3P), Mathlib slopes explicitly extracted (slope_of_Y_ne / slope_of_X_ne, addX, addY, nonsingular_add, Point.add_some) and fed to five_core_bar; the y-coordinate agreement given x-agreement is derived via Y_eq_of_X_eq with the 2P = 3P ⇒ P = 0 degenerate case refuted. Independent re-proof over 𝔽̄_p; the 𝔽_p theorem secp256k1_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff is never invoked.
does_not_follow: No P = O case (trivial, outside statement)
No roots→points existence, no ±y pairing distinctness, no #E[5](𝔽̄_p) = 25, no E[5] ≅ (ℤ/5)² — all in FiveTorsionStructure.lean per docstring, not here
Not stated via AddSubgroup.torsionBy
depends_on: Mathlib group law: nonsingular_add, Point.add_self_of_Y_ne, Point.add_some, Y_eq_of_X_eq
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop the unit-value facts on the exceptional loci: e.g. in char 7 both 1750329 and 49787136 vanish, so a 2-torsion point (y = 0) would satisfy the RHS while 5•P = P ≠ 0 — the iff would be false; the decide+transport chain is exactly what excludes p ∈ {2,3,7,...}.
prose_issues: Docstring: 'over 𝔽̄_p the twelve roots of preΨ₅ are realized by actual curve points, giving #E[5](𝔽̄_p) = 25 once paired with ±y' — not proved in this file
