table.name: seven_master_bar (private)
hypotheses: hcurve, hℓ2 (tangent slope), hℓ3 (secant slope 2P+P) — no nonzero-denominator hypotheses needed since it is an equation, not an iff
table.allowed_interpretation: The real computational content of the 7-bridge: a single polynomial identity relating the bracket G (numerator of x(4P) − x(3P) before the ℓ₄-secant) to −4(x³+7)·ψ₇(x) after clearing (ℓ₂²−3x)⁶(2y)¹². Proved by one ~4000-token sympy-designed linear_combination whose coefficients are integer numerals — hence syntactically identical to the 𝔽_p seven_master and independently re-elaborated/re-checked by the kernel over 𝔽̄_p. This is re-proof, not transport: no 𝔽_p theorem is referenced.
does_not_follow: No iff, no group law, no points; consumed only by seven_core_bar and the order-4 branch
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hcurve: y-parity terms in the certificate no longer cancel; both sides differ already at generic off-curve (x,y).

table.name: seven_core_bar (private)
hypotheses: h2, hcurve, hy
hd2 : x(2P) ≠ x (equivalently 3•P ≠ 0 at the caller)
hd3 : ℓ₃² − ℓ₂² ≠ 0 (equivalently x(3P) ≠ x, i.e. 4•P ≠ 0 at the caller)
hℓ2/hℓ3/hℓ4 slope relations
table.allowed_interpretation: x(3P) = x(4P) ⟺ ψ₇(x) = 0, as pure field algebra with slopes passed as unconstrained variables satisfying the relations; the ≠-hypotheses exclude the 3-torsion and 4-torsion x-loci, which the caller handles by separate branches. Nonvanishing of −4(x³+7) is derived internally from hcurve+hy+h2 (y ≠ 0 ⇒ x³+7 = y² ≠ 0) — this is where y ≠ 0 does double duty.
does_not_follow: Nothing when 3•P = 0 or 4•P = 0 (excluded by hd2/hd3)
No existence of ℓ₄; the caller must and does prove X3 ≠ x before forming the slope
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hd3: with ℓ₃² = ℓ₂², hℓ4 forces the S4-bracket to 0 but leaves ℓ₄ free; the LHS equality can then be toggled by choice of ℓ₄ independent of ψ₇(x) — exactly the order-4 configuration the caller treats separately.

table.name: secp256k1Bar_seven_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_seven_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
    (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (h : secp256k1Bar.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) :
    (7 : ℕ) • (Point.some x y h) = 0 ↔ (secp256k1Bar.ψ 7).evalEval x y = 0
hypotheses: h : Nonsingular x y
concrete char facts: 2, 3, 3063651608241 (= 3¹²·7⁸), 377801998336 (= 2¹⁶·7⁸), 2478758911082496 (= 2¹⁶·3⁸·7⁸) ≠ 0 in 𝔽̄_p — each a decide over ℕ in 𝔽_p transported along injective φK
table.allowed_interpretation: For every affine closure point: 7•P = 0 iff ψ 7 vanishes at (x,y). Four-way case analysis, all sides closed: (i) y = 0: both false (7P = P ≠ 0; on x³ = −7 the degree-24 polynomial is the unit 3¹²·7⁸); (ii) 3•P = 0 (via the closure 3-bridge): both false (7P = P ≠ 0; polynomial is a unit on x = 0 and x³ = −28); (iii) order-4 sub-branch X3 = x with Y3 = −y (4•P = 0): both false — 7P = 3P ≠ 0, and ψ₇(x) = 0 is refuted through seven_master_bar by showing it would force S4 = −2y = 0 against y ≠ 0; the sibling Y3 = y case is refuted (3P = P ⇒ 2P = 0 against 2P = Point.some ≠ 0); (iv) main branch 7•P = 0 ⟺ 3P = −4P ⟺ x(3P) = x(4P) via seven_core_bar, with Y_eq_of_X_eq closing the y-coordinate and the 3P = 4P degeneracy refuted. Fully independent re-proof over 𝔽̄_p; unlike the 𝔽_p file it does not use a two-torsion criterion (2•P ≠ 0 comes directly from hP2 : 2•P = Point.some X2 Y2 and Point.some_ne_zero).
does_not_follow: No P = O case
No roots→points existence, no ±y distinctness, no #E[7](𝔽̄_p) = 49, no E[7] ≅ (ℤ/7)² — delegated to SevenTorsionStructure.lean
Not phrased via AddSubgroup.torsionBy
depends_on: Mathlib group law: nonsingular_add, Point.add_some, Point.add_self_of_Y_ne/Y_eq, Y_eq_of_X_eq, slope_of_X_ne/Y_ne
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop the exceptional-locus unit facts: in char 7 all three big constants ≡ 0 and ψ₇ ≡ 7·(...) degenerates, so a 2-torsion point would satisfy the RHS while 7•P = P ≠ 0; the decide-transport chain is the concrete char ≠ 2,3,7 barrier.
prose_issues: Docstring claims the 24 roots 'are realized by actual curve points, giving #E[7](𝔽̄_p) = 49' — not proved here

table.name: secp256k1Bar_seven_torsion_iff_root
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1Bar_seven_torsion_iff_root
    (x y : AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))
    (h : secp256k1Bar.toAffine.Nonsingular x y) :
    (7 : ℕ) • Point.some x y h = 0 ↔ ((secp256k1.preΨ' 7).map φK).eval x = 0
hypotheses: h : Nonsingular x y
φK again this file's own private abbrev
table.allowed_interpretation: 7•P = 0 iff x is a root of the mapped univariate preΨ' 7 ∈ 𝔽̄_p[X] (the object whose 24 distinct roots are counted in DivisionPolynomialSeparable.lean). ψ-object: the x-only preΨ' 7, matched to the on-curve value of the bivariate ψ 7 through the closed form secp256k1_preΨ₇.
does_not_follow: Existence half (each root carries a point) absent from this file
The 2·24 + 1 = 49 count is SevenTorsionStructure.lean's job
Private φK in a public statement (same fragility as the n = 3, 5 reconciliations)
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Same shape as n = 3, 5: 'root ⇒ realized 7-torsion point' needs √(x₀³+7) ∈ 𝔽̄_p, x₀³+7 ≠ 0 at roots, and nonsingularity — none proved here.
prose_issues: Docstring 'Together they say: the 7-torsion x-locus is exactly that 24-element root set' — the composition is elsewhere

red_flags: Audit performed by source reading only (no local Lean toolchain per repo policy); every kernel_theorem classification is contingent on PR #172's CI lake build being green — the files contain no sorry/admit/axiom/native_decide in proof bodies (the only grep hits are docstring mentions of 'native_decide'), and all Bar files ARE imported by Ecdlp.lean at the PR head (lines 123-129), so they are inside both the build and the no-sorry gate.

Docstring inflation is systematic: all three module docstrings and the main-theorem docstrings assert the point counts #E[n](F̄_p) = 9/25/49 and the 'each root carries two points (x₀, ±y₀), distinct since y₀ ≠ 0' pairing — NONE of roots→points existence, ±y distinctness, O-counting, or card+exponent→(ℤ/n)² is proved in these three files; they are delegated to ThreeTorsionStructure/FiveTorsionStructure/SevenTorsionStructure.lean and TorsionStructure.lean's nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq. Anyone citing these files for the counts is citing prose, not theorems.

Public theorem statements (secp256k1Bar_{three,five,seven}_torsion_iff_root) embed the PRIVATE file-local abbrev φK; the three files each declare their own private φK, which are three distinct constants equal only up to reducible defeq — downstream files cannot name the map and must rely on definitional unfolding. Fragile API, not a soundness issue.

The characteristic hypotheses are entirely concrete: decide-checked p ∤ c for c up to 2478758911082496 against the 256-bit prime, transported through RingHom.injective φK. Sound and axiom-free, but kernel-decide on 256-bit Nat arithmetic is the same heavyweight idiom as the merged F_p files — any Mathlib change to Nat decidability performance hits six files at once.

five_core_bar is PUBLIC (matching the F_p file where five_core is also public) while the seven file makes seven_core_bar/seven_master_bar PRIVATE — inconsistent visibility for same-role certificate lemmas across the trio.

The closure 2-torsion criterion secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff exists only inside FiveTorsionBridgeBar.lean (the seven file deliberately avoids it); if a TwoTorsionPointBar/E[2]-over-closure count is added later, this is a duplication trap.

The bridges quantify over affine points only (Point.some); P = O is outside every iff. Trivial (n•O = 0) but the counting layer must add +1 for O — verified as the '+1' in the docstring arithmetic, proved elsewhere.
duplication_notes: Genuine re-proof, not transport — confirmed: no Bar theorem references its F_p point-level counterpart (secp256k1_{three,five,seven}_nsmul_eq_zero_iff are never invoked). What crosses the base change: (a) univariate closed forms via Polynomial.map + WeierstrassCurve.map_Ψ₃/map_preΨ₄ (kernel-checked), (b) constant-nonzero facts via RingHom.injective φK, (c) curve coefficients via the map_a₁..map_a₆ simp set. The group-law argument, slope extraction, and every linear_combination certificate are re-elaborated from scratch over AlgebraicClosure.

Quantified near-copy measure vs F_p originals (normalize: strip 'Bar'/'bar_', AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p) → ZMod Secp256k1.p, secp256k1Bar → secp256k1; drop comments/blanks): Three 79/118 F_p non-blank lines reappear verbatim (67%), Five 195/245 (80%), Seven 213/271 (79%). The Bar files are 1.5-1.6x longer; the surplus is (i) transported-constant lemma blocks replacing bare decides, (ii) mapped-coefficient 'simp only [secp256k1Bar, WeierstrassCurve.map, map_a₁, ..., map_zero, map_ofNat]' sets replacing the F_p one-liner 'simp only [secp256k1]', (iii) much longer docstrings. The sympy linear_combination certificates (five_core_bar's master identity, seven_master_bar) are token-identical to the F_p ones as claimed by the PR — integer-numeral coefficients, ring-generic.

Intra-trio skeleton triplication: each file re-declares private φK + φK_ne_zero (3 copies); negY extraction 3x (secp256k1Bar_negY in Three and Five — same name, legal because private — plus bar_negY in Seven); curve-equation extraction 3x in three shapes (equation_iff iff-form in Three; curve_of_nonsingular one-directional in Five and Seven); Ψ₃ closed form/eval 3x (secp256k1Bar_Ψ₃ / secp256k1Bar_Ψ₃_eval / bar_Ψ₃_closed+bar_Ψ₃_eval). A shared 'ClosureBase' file would eliminate ~120 duplicated lines and the private-φK-in-public-statement wart.

Constant-nonzero idiom drifts across the trio despite being the same pattern: Three inlines three bespoke have-blocks; Five factors the reusable private const_ne_bar (n : ℕ) (hn : ¬ p ∣ n); Seven regresses to five bespoke named lemmas (bar_two_ne_zero .. bar_c2478758911082496_ne_zero) instead of reusing a const_ne_bar. Evidence of sequential generation without consolidation.

Proof-architecture sharing: Five and Seven share the entire main-branch scaffold verbatim (s2/X2/Y2 tangent rung with hsl2/hx2val/hy2val/hns2/hP2, then s3/X3/Y3 secant rung); Seven = Five's skeleton + one more rung (s4/X4/Y4) + an extra order-4 branch (X3 = x) that Five structurally cannot hit. Seven's one deliberate deviation from its F_p original: 2•P ≠ 0 obtained directly from hP2/Point.some_ne_zero instead of a two-torsion criterion, avoiding a TwoTorsionPointBar file — while Five publicly re-proves that criterion. Also: F_p seven_master/seven_core are public, Bar versions private.

Both stacks stay in the build: the F_p bridges (ThreeTorsionBridge/FiveTorsionBridge/SevenTorsionBridge.lean, on main) and the new Bar trio coexist, giving six near-parallel files (~1600 lines) whose group-law extraction blocks must be edited in lockstep on any Mathlib WeierstrassCurve API change.
audit.summary: PR #172 adds three new files (not on main) porting the point-level n-torsion bridges from F_p to K = AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p): for every affine nonsingular point P = (x,y) of secp256k1Bar := secp256k1.map (algebraMap F_p K), n•P = 0 ⟺ (secp256k1Bar.ψ n).evalEval x y = 0, for n = 3, 5, 7, each with a reconciliation corollary restating the RHS as 'x is a root of the mapped univariate ((secp256k1.Ψ₃ / preΨ' 5 / preΨ' 7).map φK)'. The proofs are genuine independent kernel re-proofs over the closure (group-law case split, explicit Mathlib slope/addX/addY extraction, sympy linear_combination certificates re-elaborated verbatim) — not transports of the F_p theorems, which are never referenced; only polynomial closed forms (Polynomial.map), curve coefficients (map_aᵢ simp sets), and decide-checked constant-nonzero facts (p ∤ c, pushed through the injective base-change hom) cross the base change. All mandatory checks at the bridge level pass: y = 0 and n'-torsion exceptional branches are closed with both sides proved false via concrete unit values (63; 1750329, 614656, 49787136; 3¹²·7⁸, 2¹⁶·7⁸, 2¹⁶·3⁸·7⁸ ≠ 0), characteristic exclusions are concrete decide+transport rather than abstract char hypotheses, and the ψ-object is the bivariate Mathlib ψ n reduced on-curve (hcurve hypothesis required for n = 5, 7; unconditional for n = 3) to x-only closed forms. What the theorems do NOT contain — despite docstrings asserting it throughout — is everything needed for the counts: roots→points existence over the closure, (x₀,±y₀) distinctness, O-counting, #E[n] = n², and the (ℤ/n)² structure all live in other files (DivisionPolynomialSeparable, {Three,Five,Seven}TorsionStructure, TorsionStructure). The near-copy claim is accurate and quantified: after mechanical renaming, 67-80% of each F_p original's non-blank lines reappear verbatim; the trio also triplicates a private preamble (φK, negY, curve-equation, Ψ₃ closed form) with naming/idiom drift, and public reconciliation statements embed file-local private φK constants — an API fragility, not a soundness problem. Evidence class kernel_theorem throughout, contingent on the PR's CI build (files are imported by Ecdlp.lean at the PR head; no sorry/admit/axiom/native_decide present).

rows.name: secp256k1Bar_psi3_evalEval
rows.correction: Row is accurate except counterexample_if_dropped: injectivity of the base-change hom is NOT a latent hypothesis of this identity, even implicitly. The closed form transports along ANY ring hom — numeral coefficients are preserved by map_ofNat/Polynomial.map_ofNat unconditionally, and the proof (secp256k1Bar_Ψ₃ via WeierstrassCurve.map_Ψ₃ + secp256k1_Ψ₃, then ψ_three + evalEval_C) never invokes RingHom.injective. Injectivity (φK_ne_zero) is used only for the constant-nonzero transports in the point-level theorems. The identity is unconditional with no latent hypothesis at all. Everything else in the row (verbatim type, no on-curve requirement, y-freeness via ψ 3 = C Ψ₃, ambient Fact instance from Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean line 195 transitively imported via DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge) is confirmed.

rows.name: five_core_bar
rows.correction: Statement quote, hypotheses, public visibility, token-identity of the master linear_combination certificate to the F_p five_core (verified line-by-line against Ecdlp/Proved/FiveTorsionBridge.lean:62ff), and does_not_follow are all confirmed. But the counterexample_if_dropped for hd is wrong: hd (ℓ₂² − 3x ≠ 0) is logically REDUNDANT given the retained hypotheses — if ℓ₂² − 3x = 0, hℓ3 collapses to 0 = −2y, contradicting hy together with 2 ≠ 0 (which follows from h64, since 64 = 2⁶). So dropping hd alone leaves the theorem TRUE (the ℓ₂²=3x configuration is inconsistent, hence vacuous); the described 'toggle ℓ₃' counterexample only exists if hℓ3 is dropped too, which the audit's own ellipsis half-admits but then asserts anyway. Contrast with seven_core_bar's hd3, whose counterexample IS sound because hℓ4 with vanishing left factor forces only the bracket to 0 while leaving ℓ₄ free.

rows.name: secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff
rows.correction: Minor gloss imprecision only, not material: in char 2 it is the forward direction (2•P = 0 → y = 0) that fails, not the ⟸ ⟨rfl, by ring⟩ direction (y = 0 → 2P = 0 survives any characteristic); the audit's conclusion 'criterion y = 0 becomes wrong' is right. Uniqueness claim verified: git grep over the PR tree shows secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff exists and is used only in FiveTorsionBridgeBar.lean (lines 195, 266).

rows.name: seven_master_bar (private)
rows.correction: Token-identity to the F_p seven_master independently verified: statement and the entire multi-thousand-token linear_combination certificate are textually identical modulo the field type (ZMod Secp256k1.p → AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p)); Bar version is private while the F_p original is public, as the dup notes say.

rows.name: seven_core_bar (private)
rows.correction: One unnoted nuance (not an error in the row): hd2 is redundant the same way five_core_bar's hd is (hℓ3 + hy + h2 force ℓ₂² − 3x ≠ 0); the audit made no false claim about hd2, and its hd3 counterexample is genuinely sound.

missed_issues: Hypothesis non-minimality pattern missed: in five_core_bar the nondegeneracy hypothesis hd (and in seven_core_bar the analogous hd2) is derivable from the secant-slope relation hℓ3 plus hy and 2 ≠ 0 (2⁶ = 64 for the five case) — ℓ₂² − 3x = 0 makes hℓ3 read 0 = −2y. Harmless (callers discharge them anyway), but the audit instead treated hd as load-bearing and built an incorrect counterexample on it (row corrected).

API fragility not flagged: the mapped curve-equation extraction lemmas are ALL private (secp256k1Bar_equation_iff in Three, secp256k1Bar_curve_of_nonsingular in Five, bar_curve_of_nonsingular in Seven), yet the public stage-1 lemmas secp256k1Bar_psi5_evalEval and secp256k1Bar_psi7_evalEval take the raw hypothesis y ^ 2 = x ^ 3 + 7. Any downstream file starting from Nonsingular must re-derive the mapped-coefficient curve equation itself — the same private-preamble-behind-public-API wart as φK, in a fourth instance the audit did not list.

The quantified near-copy percentages are not reproducible under the audit's own stated normalization: re-implementing it (strip comments/blanks, Bar→'', AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p) → ZMod Secp256k1.p, secp256k1Bar → secp256k1) yields 87% (Three, 87/100), 90% (Five, 198/220), 84% (Seven, 214/254) of F_p non-blank non-comment lines reappearing verbatim, vs the audit's 67%/80%/79% over 118/245/271. Direction and conclusion stand — if anything the audit UNDERSTATES the duplication — but the specific figures should be labeled normalization-sensitive estimates, not measurements. The 1.5–1.6x file-length claim is exact (measured 1.65/1.53/1.59).

The 'never references its F_p counterpart' claim is confirmed at proof level, but the audit did not note that FiveTorsionBridgeBar.lean line 193 DOES name secp256k1_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff in a docstring (prose citation of what is being ported) — worth stating so a later grep-based reviewer is not misled into thinking a proof dependency was missed.
verify.overall: The audit table is accurate and unusually rigorous: 13 of 15 rows CONFIRMED, 2 CORRECTED, none REFUTED, and both corrections are confined to the secondary counterexample_if_dropped field, not to any statement quote, hypothesis list, evidence class, or does_not_follow analysis. Every quoted exact_type was checked verbatim against git show 5f61fa5 and matched, including the full seven_master_bar bracket identity. Independently re-verified: (a) all three Bar files are new (absent from main ac1ec94) and imported by Ecdlp.lean at PR-head lines 123/126/128, inside the build and no-sorry gate; (b) the only sorry/admit/axiom/native_decide grep hits are docstring mentions of native_decide; (c) the Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance lives at Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean:195, imported transitively; (d) secp256k1Bar is secp256k1.map (algebraMap ...) at DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean:163; (e) F_p five_core, seven_master, seven_core are public while Bar seven versions are private — the visibility-inconsistency red flag is correct; (f) the F_p seven bridge does use secp256k1_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff (line 147) while the Bar seven file replaces it with the hP2/Point.some_ne_zero route, per its own source comment; (g) the seven_master certificate is token-identical between F_p and Bar; (h) the closure 2-torsion criterion exists only in FiveTorsionBridgeBar; (i) the Structure/Separable delegation targets (ThreeTorsionStructure/FiveTorsionStructure/SevenTorsionStructure/DivisionPolynomialSeparable with the _roots_card_bar/_roots_nodup_bar lemmas, secp256k1_preΨ₅ in CoprimePsi3Psi5, secp256k1_preΨ₇ in CoprimePsi3Psi7, secp256k1_preΨ₄ in FourDivisionPolynomial) all exist at the PR head. The docstring-inflation red flag is verified word-for-word: all three module and main-theorem docstrings assert roots→points realization, ±y pairing, and the 9/25/49 counts, none of which these files prove. The two corrections: row psi3_evalEval falsely calls hom-injectivity a latent hypothesis of an identity that needs none, and row five_core_bar asserts a counterexample for dropping hd when hd is actually redundant given hℓ3+hy+h64 (dropping it leaves the theorem true). The only red-flag caveat: the 67/80/79% near-copy figures are not reproducible — my re-implementation of the stated normalization gives 84-90%, so the duplication is if anything understated; the qualitative duplication_notes are otherwise all confirmed, including the triplicated private preamble with naming drift (const_ne_bar factored in Five, bespoke lemmas in Three and Seven) and the six-file lockstep-maintenance exposure.

table.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq_of_module
table.exact_type: theorem nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq_of_module {n : ℕ} [hp : Fact n.Prime] (A : Type*) [AddCommGroup A] [Module (ZMod n) A] (hcard : Nat.card A = n ^ 2) : Nonempty (A ≃+ ZMod n × ZMod n)
hypotheses: Fact n.Prime (instance)
Module (ZMod n) A supplied as an honest instance parameter
Nat.card A = n ^ 2 (this alone forces A finite and nonempty via Nat.card_pos_iff; Finite/Fintype are DERIVED inside the proof by Fintype.ofFinite, not assumed)
table.allowed_interpretation: Any ZMod-n vector space (n prime) of cardinality exactly n^2 is additively isomorphic to (ZMod n)^2. Proof: Module.card_eq_pow_finrank gives n^2 = n^finrank, Nat.pow_right_injective (2 ≤ n) pins finrank = 2, LinearEquiv.ofFinrankEq classifies, .toAddEquiv forgets scalars.
does_not_follow: Nothing for composite n (Fact n.Prime is load-bearing: |A|=16 killed by 4 admits Z/2×Z/2×Z/4 ≢ Z/4×Z/4)
Nothing for card n^1, n^3, or unknown card
No canonical/natural equivalence: Nonempty only, basis chosen noncomputably
No compatibility with any extra structure (Galois action, pairings)
depends_on: Mathlib: Module.card_eq_pow_finrank, LinearEquiv.ofFinrankEq, Nat.pow_right_injective, Nat.card_pos_iff, Module.Finite.of_finite
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Drop hcard: A = ZMod n (card n, killed by n) is not ≃+ (ZMod n)^2. Drop primality: n=4, A = Z/2 × Z/2 × Z/4.

table.name: Ecdlp.Torsion.nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq
table.exact_type: theorem nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq {A : Type*} [acg : AddCommGroup A] {n : ℕ} [hp : Fact n.Prime] (hkill : ∀ a : A, n • a = 0) (hcard : Nat.card A = n ^ 2) : Nonempty (A ≃+ ZMod n × ZMod n)
hypotheses: Fact n.Prime
hkill : ∀ a, n • a = 0 (exponent divides n)
hcard : Nat.card A = n ^ 2
table.allowed_interpretation: N10(iii): finite abelian group of order n^2 killed by prime n is (Z/n)^2. Builds the ZMod n module structure from hkill via AddCommGroup.zmodModule and applies the _of_module core with fully explicit @-application. This is the exact lemma invoked by all three per-ℓ structure theorems, and at every call site BOTH hypotheses are genuinely discharged: hkill by (fun a => AddSubgroup.torsionBy.nsmul a) — definitional for a torsionBy subgroup — and hcard by the named card theorem .trans (by norm_num) turning 9/25/49 into 3^2/5^2/7^2.
does_not_follow: This is 'cardinality + exponent ⇒ structure', pure group theory: it does NOT prove #E[n] = n^2 for any curve (that is the per-ℓ counting chain, or open N10(i)/(ii) for general n)
Does not use or provide the structure theorem for finite abelian groups
Drop hkill: Z/9 has card 3^2 but is cyclic, not (Z/3)^2
table.counterexample_if_dropped: hkill dropped: A = ZMod (n^2) (cyclic of order n^2, not killed by n) is not ≃+ ZMod n × ZMod n.

table.name: secp256k1Bar_isElliptic (instance)
table.exact_type: instance secp256k1Bar_isElliptic : secp256k1Bar.IsElliptic := inferInstanceAs ((secp256k1.map φcl).IsElliptic)
hypotheses: Definitional: secp256k1Bar := secp256k1.map (algebraMap (ZMod Secp256k1.p) (AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p))) (DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean:163), and φcl is that same algebraMap, so inferInstanceAs is sound
Mathlib instance: IsUnit Δ transports along W.map f
table.allowed_interpretation: The base-changed curve is elliptic; this is the instance that makes exists_nonsingular_y applicable. Registered ONLY here; FiveTorsionStructure and SevenTorsionStructure import ThreeTorsionStructure solely to inherit it.
does_not_follow: Nothing about torsion; pure plumbing
prose_issues: Architecturally misplaced: an instance about secp256k1Bar living in a 3-torsion file forces the n=5/7 files to import the n=3 file

audit.summary: PR #172's three structure-assembly files each prove, over F-bar_p = AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p) and for l in {3,5,7}: (a) the l-torsion of secp256k1Bar (:= secp256k1.map algebraMap, so genuinely the closure curve) is exactly the set {O} + liftP''roots + (-liftP)''roots, (b) Set.ncard / Nat.card / torsionBy-card = l^2 as numerals (9/25/49), and (c) Nonempty (torsionBy Point ((l:N):Z) equiv+ ZMod l x ZMod l). The counting chain is sound as written: card = 1 + 2*((l^2-1)/2) via an explicit Finset (insert 0 of two disjoint images), with injectivity by x-projection, disjointness from y != -y (char != 2 by decide p-not-dvd-2, y != 0 from a transported Bezout coprimality Psi2Sq-perp-division-polynomial), O counted by the insert and excluded from images by Point.some_ne_zero, and the roots-to-points direction honestly proved (exists_nonsingular_y over IsAlgClosed + the bridge iffs quoted verbatim from the BridgeBar files, both mp and mpr used). No Fintype/Finite instance on the point type is assumed anywhere: Set.ncard/Nat.card are used and finiteness inside N10(iii) is derived from Nat.card = n^2 > 0. The N10(iii) lemma nonempty_addEquiv_zmod_prod_of_card_eq_sq (main, TorsionStructure.lean) has hypotheses [Fact n.Prime], hkill, hcard, and all three are genuinely discharged at each call site (Nat.prime_three / norm_num; AddSubgroup.torsionBy.nsmul; named card theorem .trans norm_num). What the theorems do NOT say: anything about E(F_p)[l] over the base field (where l-torsion is trivial for secp256k1), any uniform-n statement, any explicit/canonical isomorphism (Nonempty of a noncomputable choice), or anything about the 256-bit group order or ECDLP. Main caveats: the whole chain transitively rests on native_decide certificates (squarefree/separability + Bezout cofactors), contradicting a literal reading of the per-file 'no native_decide' prose, and SevenTorsionStructure's header falsely claims the file contains no decide; char != l is implicit in imported concrete facts, never a hypothesis. The three files are near-verbatim copies of one 18-role skeleton whose only per-l inputs are {bridge iff, root-count pair, coprimality cert, prime fact} — ready for the planned generic API.

Retired-set phrasing fragility: the generated retired strings are exactly 'N ledger rows', 'N rows', and '~M distinct' — a stale figure phrased '209 distinct' (no tilde), '248-row ledger', 'rows: 248', or split across markup survives the scan. The tilde is load-bearing in the distinct pattern. The auditor noted the whitelist limitation but not that the retired matching is exact-substring and phrasing-sensitive.

Higher-figure fail-open: CANON_RE/ALT_RE use re.search (first match wins) and the retired set only covers figures BELOW the current ones. A leftover or duplicate canonical-format line with HIGHER figures (e.g. from a reverted count bump) placed after the true canonical line in VERIFIED.md — or any '265 ledger rows' claim in a whitelisted doc — is invisible to every gate: not first-matched, not retired. The machinery only defends against stale-low figures, not stale-high ones.

No table-identity anchoring: count_ledger_rows counts ANY markdown row above '### Coverage restatements' whose last cell starts with 'proved', regardless of which table it belongs to; and on mismatch the gate's own error message instructs 'update the canonical line' to the inflated recount. A prose/status table added above the cutoff with a 'proved…'-prefixed last column would inflate the headline with full gate blessing. The auditor's row-1 counterexample attributes stray-table protection to startswith('| '), which does not protect against stray tables above the cutoff — only against non-table lines.

archive/docs/ contains multiple would-be-retired headline strings at PR head ('242 ledger rows / ~204 distinct kernel-verified results' in ONE_PAGE_SUMMARY.md, '220 ledger rows / ~184 distinct' in PLATFORM_STRATEGY.md, '≈114 ledger rows / ~105' in REVIEW_DOSSIER.md). Presumably intentionally out of scope as historical archive, but the audit never states that the whitelist scoping excludes archive/, notes/, tasks/, and PUBLISHABLE_UNITS.md — the last three currently carry no count strings (verified), archive/ does.

CI-wiring nuance the audit left implicit but that holds up: check_counts.py and check_status_consistency.py run in ci.yml on every push AND pull_request, and docs-sync.yml drift-gates the PURE artifacts (stats.json, badge, STATUS.md, knowledge_graph, COVERAGE.md) on PRs while dashboard.html/index.html are explicitly EXCLUDED from the PR drift gate (SITE artifacts, regenerated on main only) — so on a PR the dashboard/index counters are checked only by check_status_consistency's substring assertions, not by regeneration.
verify.overall: The audit table is accurate on every load-bearing claim and I could reproduce all of its executional evidence independently: the three counting scripts (plus check_status_consistency.py) have byte-identical blob hashes on origin/main ac1ec94 and PR-head 5f61fa5 (989f8ad4 / 6e445781 / b141b4bc / c1a7d169), so #172 changes counted DATA, never counting RULES. In a PR-head worktree I re-ran all four gates myself: check_counts.py exit 0 (recount 260 / ~221), gen_stats.py --check in sync, gen_status.py regeneration byte-identical to the committed STATUS.md, check_status_consistency.py OK. stats.json values (260/221/39/125/242), the badge transition '209 (248 rows)' → '221 (260 rows)', STATUS.md 248/209/112 → 260/221/125, the exactly-12 added bare-'| proved |' rows above the cutoff, the frozen alt figure 39, and both discountable-row observations (ρ-descent supporting row sharing Ecdlp/Proved/NormEDSConsecutiveZeros.lean with its consumer theorem; ThreeTorsionBridgeBar's verbatim 'token-identical' self-description) all check out, as does the central structural finding: the anti-inflation invariant is arithmetic-only, so the +12 distinct increment (and the public badge) rests on an unchecked curation call with roughly ±2 results of latitude. Corrections are secondary: row 1's counterexample is wrong (dropping HEADLINE_END changes nothing today — the tier-0 restatements are prose, not proved-status rows; recount is 260 with or without the break), rows 4/7 give an incomplete 13-modules→12-rows accounting (2 folds − would give 11 — plus the NormEDS 1-module→2-rows split nets 12, and that split IS the flagged supporting row, so the 'fold' mitigation partially cancels rather than offsets), and row 6 echoes gen_status.py's 'never hand-typed' docstring despite two hard-coded '486' numerals in the template. The audit also missed the sharpest live gap: explore.html's stale '228 ledger rows' KPI (present on main and PR head) escapes every gate — not whitelisted in check_counts, not read by check_status_consistency, not regenerated by docs-sync — plus the phrasing fragility (tilde-dependent '~M distinct'), the stale-HIGH-figure fail-open, and the absence of table-identity anchoring above the cutoff. None of the missed items changes the verdict on #172 itself: the PR is fully self-consistent under unchanged, correctly-functioning rules, and the audit's inflation-latitude finding stands, slightly sharpened.

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Φ₃
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_Φ₃ :
    secp256k1.Φ 3 = X * secp256k1.Ψ₃ ^ 2 - secp256k1.preΨ₄ * secp256k1.Ψ₂Sq :=
  secp256k1.Φ_three
hypotheses: none — no [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p)], no characteristic hypotheses; unconditional polynomial identity in (ZMod Secp256k1.p)[X]
table.allowed_interpretation: The Mathlib division polynomial Φ 3 of the concrete curve secp256k1 (WeierstrassCurve (ZMod Secp256k1.p), a₁..a₄=0, a₆=7) equals X·Ψ₃² − preΨ₄·Ψ₂Sq as elements of (ZMod Secp256k1.p)[X]. This is a zero-content specialization: the proof term is literally the Mathlib lemma WeierstrassCurve.Φ_three applied to secp256k1.
does_not_follow: Nothing about points, torsion, roots, or x(3•P) — this is coefficient bookkeeping only; the point-level formula lives exclusively in #172's secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃.
No new mathematics beyond Mathlib: it is a named alias of WeierstrassCurve.Φ_three; #172 uses the same Mathlib lemma inline (rw [secp256k1.Φ_three]) and never needs this declaration.
Name-level unique to #173 (no declaration named secp256k1_Φ₃ exists on main or in #172), but content-level subsumed by Mathlib + #172's private Φ₃_eval derivation.
Does not state the fully-expanded coefficient form Φ 3 = X⁹ − 672X⁶ + ... (no such coefficient-level theorem exists in this module, unlike main's secp256k1_Φ₂).
table.counterexample_if_dropped: n/a — no hypotheses to drop; the identity is the definitional recurrence Φ n = X·Ψₙ² − Ψₙ₊₁·Ψₙ₋₁ at n=3 specialized via b₂=b₄=b₈=0, b₆=28.
prose_issues: Module docstring claims this file is 'the degree half of node N10(i) (deg [n] = n²)' groundwork — no degree (natDegree) statement is proved anywhere in the module; the degree fact for Φ n is already on main (NumeratorDominates.lean, secp256k1_Φ_natDegree), not here.

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_ΨSq₃
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_ΨSq₃ : secp256k1.ΨSq 3 = secp256k1.Ψ₃ ^ 2 :=
  secp256k1.ΨSq_three
hypotheses: none — unconditional polynomial identity in (ZMod Secp256k1.p)[X]
table.allowed_interpretation: For the concrete secp256k1 curve, Mathlib's ΨSq 3 equals Ψ₃² in (ZMod Secp256k1.p)[X]. Proof term is exactly Mathlib's WeierstrassCurve.ΨSq_three specialized; zero curve-specific content (holds for every Weierstrass curve over every commutative ring).
does_not_follow: Nothing point-level; no claim that ΨSq 3 vanishes exactly on 3-torsion x-coordinates (that bridge is main's secp256k1_three_nsmul_eq_zero_iff via ψ₃, and #172's structure files).
Name-level unique to #173; content-level subsumed by Mathlib itself — #172's private ΨSq₃_eval rewrites with secp256k1.ΨSq_three directly and does not need this alias.
No degree-8 (natDegree) claim despite the docstring's 'degree 8 = 3² − 1' framing.
table.counterexample_if_dropped: n/a — no hypotheses.
prose_issues: Docstring calls it 'the n = 3 denominator of the multiplication formula' — the multiplication formula itself is not in this PR; without #172 nothing in the merged tree connects ΨSq 3 to x(3•P).

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_ΨSq₃_eval
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_ΨSq₃_eval (x : ZMod Secp256k1.p) :
    (secp256k1.ΨSq 3).eval x = 9 * x ^ 8 + 504 * x ^ 5 + 7056 * x ^ 2
hypotheses: x : ZMod Secp256k1.p (universally quantified evaluation point in F_p)
no primality Fact, no characteristic hypothesis
table.allowed_interpretation: Pointwise evaluation identity: for every x in F_p, (ΨSq 3).eval x = 9x⁸ + 504x⁵ + 7056x² (= (3x⁴+84x)² = (Ψ₃(x))², consistent with secp256k1_ΨSq₃). A family of scalar identities in F_p, one per x.
does_not_follow: STATEMENT-LEVEL DUPLICATE of #172: proposition is verbatim identical to #172 TripleMultiplicationFormula.lean's `private theorem ΨSq₃_eval` (same binder, same RHS); proofs differ only in routing (via secp256k1_ΨSq₃ vs direct rw [secp256k1.ΨSq_three]). #172's copy is file-private, so #173's is the only exported version.

Pointwise eval does NOT assert the coefficient-level identity secp256k1.ΨSq 3 = 9X⁸ + 504X⁵ + 7056X² — over the finite field F_p, agreement of eval at every point does not in general imply polynomial equality (X^p − X ≡ 0 pointwise), and no coefficient-level expanded form is stated in this module.
No natDegree = 8 claim follows from this theorem's type.
Evaluation is over F_p only — says nothing about values or roots over AlgebraicClosure (ZMod Secp256k1.p); #172's Bar files handle the closure, #173 never touches it.
No root-counting, no torsion content, no y-coordinate involvement.
table.counterexample_if_dropped: If misread as the coefficient identity: over F_p pointwise-everywhere equality is weaker than polynomial equality (e.g. X^p − X evaluates to 0 at every x yet is a nonzero polynomial); the coefficient form happens to be true here but is not what this theorem says.
prose_issues: Docstring: 'the explicit degree-8 denominator of x(3•P)' — neither the degree nor the role as denominator of x(3•P) is part of the type; the latter is proved only in #172.

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_Φ₃_eval
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_Φ₃_eval (x : ZMod Secp256k1.p) :
    (secp256k1.Φ 3).eval x = x ^ 9 - 672 * x ^ 6 + 2352 * x ^ 3 + 21952
hypotheses: x : ZMod Secp256k1.p
no primality Fact, no characteristic hypothesis
table.allowed_interpretation: Pointwise evaluation identity: for every x in F_p, (Φ 3).eval x = x⁹ − 672x⁶ + 2352x³ + 21952, obtained by substituting the eval forms of Ψ₃ (3x⁴+84x), preΨ₄ (2x⁶+280x³−784), Ψ₂Sq (4x³+28) into Φ 3 = X·Ψ₃² − preΨ₄·Ψ₂Sq and closing with ring.
does_not_follow: STATEMENT-LEVEL DUPLICATE of #172: proposition is verbatim identical to #172 TripleMultiplicationFormula.lean's `private theorem Φ₃_eval`, whose proof body is byte-for-byte the same tactic script except it rewrites secp256k1.Φ_three directly. Two independent kernel proofs of one proposition; only #173's is public.

Does NOT state the coefficient-level identity secp256k1.Φ 3 = X⁹ − 672X⁶ + 2352X³ + 21952 (see finite-field caveat above), and does NOT prove natDegree (Φ 3) = 9 — the docstring's 'degree 9 = 3² matches deg [n] = n²' is commentary, not a consequence of this type (the degree fact is main's secp256k1_Φ_natDegree in NumeratorDominates.lean).
No connection to x(3•P) is proved in this PR: without #172, the merged tree contains the numerator/denominator eval forms but no theorem that x(3•P) equals their quotient.
F_p only; no algebraic-closure statement; no roots→points transition anywhere in this module (none claimed at type level).
table.counterexample_if_dropped: n/a — no droppable hypotheses; the only misreading risk is eval-form vs coefficient-form (see ΨSq₃_eval row).
prose_issues: Docstring asserts the degree-9 = 3² interpretation which the theorem does not prove.

Module docstring 'together with Ψ₃ ⊥ Φ₃ (coprimality, node N5 in progress) this is the n = 3 instance of the lowest-terms degree bookkeeping' — the coprimality is not in this PR (it arrives as #172's secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq / secp256k1Bar_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq in DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean); honest about 'in progress' but the 'lowest-terms' framing is aspirational.

red_flags: Audit is source-reading only (no local Lean toolchain, no .lake checkout of the pinned Mathlib rev fabf563a): the existence and exact statements of Mathlib's WeierstrassCurve.Φ_three / ΨSq_three could not be kernel-verified locally. Corroboration: main already builds files using the sibling API (Φ_two in MultiplicationFormula.lean, ΨSq/Φ in DivisionPolynomialEvalBridge.lean), and both #172 and #173 use Φ_three/ΨSq_three identically. verify.overall: High-quality, essentially accurate audit; verified adversarially against the actual sources of origin/claude/agi-final-technology-usdtmk (PR #173), 5f61fa5 (PR #172), and origin/main. All four exact_type quotes are verbatim; the hypothesis lists are complete (the module genuinely has no Fact-primality or characteristic hypotheses — all four theorems are unconditional identities/evaluations in/over ZMod Secp256k1.p); evidence_class kernel_theorem is right for all four (modulo the honestly-disclosed inability to kernel-check locally, corroborated by main's existing use of the sibling Mathlib API, e.g. WeierstrassCurve.Φ_two at MultiplicationFormula.lean:34). The central findings all reproduce: proposition-identical private duplicates in #172's TripleMultiplicationFormula.lean; #172 strictly subsuming #173 (its point-level secp256k1_triple_x_eq_Φ₃_div_ΨSq₃ with the y=0 branch via main's secp256k1_isCoprime_Ψ₂Sq_Ψ₃ Bézout certificate); zero name collisions in either merge order (git grep for secp256k1_Φ₃/secp256k1_ΨSq₃ empty on both main and #172); disjoint Ecdlp.lean hunks; conflicts confined to regenerated artifacts; docstring overselling (degree and x(3•P) claims are prose, not types). The eval-vs-coefficient-form gap (pointwise equality over F_p is weaker than polynomial equality) is correctly identified and is the strongest 'what it does not say' point; the audit does not overstate it. Verdicts: 3 CONFIRMED, 1 CORRECTED — row secp256k1_Φ₃'s counterexample_if_dropped wrongly claims the identity is 'specialized via b₂=b₄=b₈=0, b₆=28' when WeierstrassCurve.Φ_three is curve-generic and the proof uses no b-invariants (they enter only the *_eval theorems). Missed items are minor: BARRIERS.md omitted from the shared-artifact conflict list; the DivisionPolynomial import is as vestigial as the flagged FourDivisionPolynomial one; a Fact-variable caveat on the cited main-side degree lemma.

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_natDegree
table.exact_type: theorem secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_natDegree : (secp256k1.preΨ' 11).natDegree = 60
hypotheses: None in the statement.
Internally: h11 : ((11 : ℕ) : ZMod Secp256k1.p) ≠ 0, discharged by native_decide (p ∤ 11) — the char ≠ 11 condition is present but hidden in the proof.

Global instance Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.p) (Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean, itself native_decide-backed Pratt certificate) is in scope via imports; Mathlib's exact-degree lemma natDegree_preΨ' needs the domain/nontriviality this provides. The docstring's 'the only hypothesis is 11 ≠ 0 in 𝔽_p' elides this.
table.allowed_interpretation: The Mathlib polynomial preΨ' 11 of the concrete secp256k1 Weierstrass curve over F_p has natDegree exactly 60 = (11²−1)/2.
does_not_follow: Anything about points of E or the torsion subgroup E[11].

That preΨ' 11 equals the classical 11-division polynomial ψ₁₁/Ψ₁₁ — the identification 'preΨ' 11 *is* the genuine 11-division polynomial' is asserted in prose; no Mathlib lemma (e.g. Ψ vs preΨ for odd index) is invoked in this file.
Any statement over AlgebraicClosure (F_p-bar); the polynomial lives over F_p.
table.counterexample_if_dropped: If (11 : F_p) = 0 (i.e. p = 11), the leading coefficient of preΨ' 11 vanishes and natDegree < 60 — the internal native_decide hypothesis is load-bearing.
prose_issues: Docstring headline '**deg (preΨ' 11) = 60** (11-torsion bound)' — the parenthetical '(11-torsion bound)' claims a torsion interpretation the type does not contain. Safe: '(degree of the odd-index 11-division polynomial over 𝔽_p)'.
Docstring 'the only hypothesis is 11 ≠ 0 in 𝔽_p' — also requires the global Fact (Nat.Prime p) domain instance.

Module docstring 'so its roots are exactly the x-coordinates of the order-11 points' — unproved for ℓ=11 anywhere in the repo (bridges exist only for ℓ=2,3,5,7). Safe replacement: 'classically its roots over the algebraic closure are the x-coordinates of the order-11 points; that roots→points bridge is NOT formalized for ℓ = 11 (the repo's point-level bridges stop at ℓ = 7)'.

Module docstring 'Hence secp256k1 has at most 60 nontrivial 11-torsion x-coordinates, i.e. #E[11] ≤ 121 — consistent with the classical structure E[11] ≅ (ℤ/11)² (120 nontrivial points, ±y per x ⇒ 60 x-coords)' — none of this is proved; the ±y-per-x halving additionally assumes y ≠ 0 and (x,y) ≠ (x,−y) (char ≠ 2) and counts O, all unformalized. Safe: 'Hence preΨ' 11 has at most 60 roots in 𝔽_p counted with multiplicity. No statement about torsion points or #E[11] is proved here; E[11] ≅ (ℤ/11)² over the closure is explicitly-unproved classical motivation only.'
Module docstring 'A concrete named level of the uniform secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le (#E[n] ≤ n²)' — the gloss '#E[n] ≤ n²' misstates the uniform lemma, which is also only an F_p root-count bound.

Module docstring 'All polynomial/degree facts over 𝔽_p; no axioms' — the proof uses native_decide, which adds the compiler-trust axiom (Lean.ofReduceBool) to the theorem's axiom footprint; 'no axioms' should read 'no new axiom declarations; native_decide extends trust to the Lean compiler'.

table.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_eleven_torsion_x_card_le
table.exact_type:
theorem secp256k1_eleven_torsion_x_card_le :
    Multiset.card (secp256k1.preΨ' 11).roots ≤ 60
hypotheses: None in the statement; Polynomial.roots requires the IsDomain instance on ZMod Secp256k1.p, supplied by the global Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance.
Proof = Polynomial.card_roots' composed with the degree theorem, so inherits native_decide.
table.field_of_statement: F_p (ZMod Secp256k1.p) — roots multiset IN F_p, with multiplicity; NOT AlgebraicClosure
table.allowed_interpretation: The multiset of roots of preΨ' 11 in F_p (counted with multiplicity) has cardinality at most 60. In particular preΨ' 11 has at most 60 distinct roots in F_p.
does_not_follow: That these roots are x-coordinates of order-11 points: no root↔torsion bridge for ℓ=11 exists anywhere at the PR head (verified by repo-wide grep — preΨ' 11 appears only in this file; point-level bridges exist only for ℓ=2,3,5,7, e.g. secp256k1_seven_nsmul_eq_zero_iff).

#E[11] ≤ 121: E[11] ≅ (ℤ/11)² lives over the algebraic closure; a root count over F_p says nothing about the closure root count, so even with a bridge the geometric bound would not follow from this type.

Any statement about E(F_p)[11]: classically E(F_p)[11] = {O} (since #E(F_p) = n is a prime ≠ 11), so the 'at most 60 eleven-torsion x-coordinates over F_p' reading is not only unproved but would be vacuously weak — the honest F_p-rational count is 0 nontrivial points; that fact is also not proved here.
E[11] ≅ (ℤ/11)², the ±y pairing (needs y ≠ 0 and char ≠ 2 distinctness of (x,y),(x,−y)), and the '+1 for O' arithmetic behind '121'.
table.counterexample_if_dropped: Without the domain instance the bound itself can fail in spirit: over a non-domain like ZMod 8, X² − 1 has 4 roots > deg 2; and dropping p ∤ 11 breaks the degree-60 input.
prose_issues: Docstring '**At most 60 eleven-torsion x-coordinates.** ... these are exactly the x-coordinates of the order-11 points, so secp256k1 has at most 60 nontrivial 11-torsion x-coordinates (#E[11] ≤ 121, consistent with E[11] ≅ (ℤ/11)²)' — every clause after 'at most 60 roots in 𝔽_p' is unproved. Safe: '**At most 60 roots of preΨ' 11 in 𝔽_p** (with multiplicity): degree-60 polynomial ⇒ ≤ 60 roots over 𝔽_p. The roots↔order-11-points bridge (proved for ℓ = 3, 5, 7 in the *TorsionBridge files) is NOT formalized for ℓ = 11, and no bound on #E[11] — which lives over the algebraic closure — is proved.'

VERIFIED.md row 156: '**≤ 60 eleven-torsion x-coordinates** (#E[11]≤121; roots of the odd division polynomial ψ₁₁ are the order-11 x-coords, consistent with E[11]≅(ℤ/11)²; the concrete n=11 level of the uniform odd bound)' — replace with '**≤ 60 roots of preΨ' 11 in 𝔽_p (with multiplicity)** (degree-60 root bound over 𝔽_p only; roots↔order-11-points bridge and any #E[11] statement NOT formalized; instance of secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le at n=11)'.
The theorem NAME itself ('eleven_torsion_x_card_le') encodes the unproved torsion interpretation; a type-honest name is secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_roots_card_le.
VERIFIED.md rows 155/156 method column says 'Mathlib' but the proofs factor through the native_decide-backed degree theorem — trust chain understated ('Mathlib + native_decide' would be accurate).

VERIFIED.md rows 158/159 method column 'Mathlib' understates the native_decide dependency inherited from the degree theorem.

red_flags: Roots→points transition ASSERTED, never proved, for ℓ=11 and ℓ=13: both module docstrings say the roots 'are exactly the x-coordinates of the order-ℓ points', and VERIFIED.md rows 156/159 repeat it as if machine-checked. Repo-wide grep at 810de34 confirms preΨ' 11 / preΨ' 13 occur ONLY in ElevenTorsion.lean / ThirteenTorsion.lean; point-level bridges (n•P = 0 ↔ ψₙ vanishes) exist only for ℓ = 2, 3, 5, 7 (TwoTorsionPoint, ThreeTorsionBridge, FiveTorsionBridge, SevenTorsionBridge).

Field confusion baked into the prose: the proved root bounds are over F_p (Multiset.card of Polynomial.roots in ZMod Secp256k1.p), but the advertised conclusions (#E[11] ≤ 121, #E[13] ≤ 169, E[ℓ] ≅ (ℤ/ℓ)²) live over the algebraic closure. An F_p root count cannot bound closure roots, so the geometric claims would not follow even if a bridge existed. Worse, over F_p itself the classical truth is E(F_p)[11] = E(F_p)[13] = {O} (group order n is a ~2^256 prime, ≠ 11, 13), so the 'at most 60/84 ℓ-torsion x-coordinates' reading is both unproved and nearly vacuous.

Theorem NAMES encode the unproved interpretation: secp256k1_eleven_torsion_x_card_le / secp256k1_thirteen_torsion_x_card_le name torsion while their types mention only polynomial roots. Recommend renaming to secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_roots_card_le / secp256k1_preΨ₁₃_roots_card_le (or at minimum an explicit 'no torsion bridge for this ℓ' caveat in the docstring).

Ledger method column understates trust: rows 155, 156, 158, 159 say 'Mathlib' but every one of those proofs factors through the native_decide-backed natDegree theorems (and the Pratt-certificate Fact p.Prime instance, also native_decide). Module docstrings' 'no axioms' is likewise imprecise — native_decide adds the compiler-trust axiom (Lean.ofReduceBool) to the axiom footprint.
The 'ψ₁₁ = Ψ₁₁ = preΨ' 11' (resp. 13) identification ('odd index ⇒ no ψ₂ factor ... *is* the genuine division polynomial') is prose only; no Mathlib factorisation lemma is invoked in either file.

Arithmetic glosses ('120 nontrivial points, ±y per x ⇒ 60 x-coords', '#E[ℓ] ≤ ℓ²' including O) silently assume y ≠ 0 for order-ℓ points, (x,y) ≠ (x,−y) (char ≠ 2), and O-counting — none formalized in these files.

Count inflation: six new VERIFIED.md rows plus a badges/theorems.json bump for two files whose entire content is a decide/native_decide-level instantiation of the already-merged uniform lemmas in Ecdlp/Proved/OddTorsionBound.lean.
duplication_notes: ElevenTorsion.lean and ThirteenTorsion.lean are line-for-line near-copies of each other (only n, the degree constant, and cross-reference names differ) and structural copies of SevenTorsion.lean / FiveTorsion.lean. The over-claiming 'roots are exactly the x-coordinates of order-ℓ points' prose was copied from the ℓ = 5/7 modules — where a point-level bridge at least exists elsewhere in the repo — into rungs where NO bridge exists, silently upgrading unbridged rungs to bridged-sounding prose.

Neither new file imports or uses Ecdlp/Proved/OddTorsionBound.lean, yet all six theorems are one-line instances of it: secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_natDegree = secp256k1_odd_preΨ_natDegree (hodd := by decide) (hn := by native_decide) at n = 11 (modulo evaluating (11²−1)/2), and secp256k1_eleven_torsion_x_card_le = secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le at n = 11; same for 13. Each file instead re-instantiates Mathlib's natDegree_preΨ' from scratch, duplicating the proof pattern of SevenTorsion.lean.

Marker recommendation: YES — all six VERIFIED.md rows (lines 154-159) should carry an explicit non-headline instance marker, e.g. append '*instance of `secp256k1_odd_preΨ_natDegree` / `secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le` at n = 11 (resp. 13)*' (matching the ledger's existing italic-caveat style used for the generic-group and security rows), or collapse the six rows into two 'n = 11, 13 instances of the uniform odd bound' rows. They should not increment the headline theorem count in badges/theorems.json, since the mathematical content was already merged with OddTorsionBound.lean; the genuinely new artifacts are only the named constants 60 and 84.
audit.summary: PR #174's two new modules (Ecdlp/Proved/ElevenTorsion.lean, Ecdlp/Proved/ThirteenTorsion.lean at 810de34) contain six kernel-checked theorems whose types prove exactly: natDegree (preΨ' 11) = 60, natDegree (preΨ' 13) = 84, both polynomials nonzero, and Multiset.card of their roots over F_p (ZMod Secp256k1.p, with multiplicity) ≤ 60 / ≤ 84. The proofs are sound (Mathlib natDegree_preΨ' + card_roots', with p ∤ 11 / p ∤ 13 via native_decide and the global Pratt-certificate Fact p.Prime instance). However, both module docstrings, the two *_torsion_x_card_le / *_ne_zero docstrings, and VERIFIED.md rows 154-159 systematically over-claim: they assert the roots ARE the x-coordinates of order-ℓ points and derive #E[11] ≤ 121 / #E[13] ≤ 169 'consistent with E[ℓ] ≅ (ℤ/ℓ)²'. No root↔torsion bridge exists for ℓ = 11 or 13 anywhere at the PR head (bridges stop at ℓ = 7), the proved root counts are over F_p while E[ℓ] lives over the algebraic closure, and y ≠ 0 / (x,−y)-distinctness / O-counting behind the '±y per x' arithmetic are all unformalized. Every flagged sentence should be narrowed to 'degree/root-count bound over F_p' with E[ℓ] ≅ (ℤ/ℓ)² allowed only as explicitly-unproved motivation; the theorem names should ideally drop 'torsion'; the ledger method column for the four downstream rows should say 'Mathlib + native_decide'; and all six ledger rows should carry a non-headline instance marker (they are decide-level instantiations of the already-merged secp256k1_odd_preΨ_natDegree / secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le in OddTorsionBound.lean) rather than counting as new headline theorems.

rows.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_natDegree
rows.correction: Row is right about everything except the Fact (Nat.Prime p) dependency. Verified verbatim: exact_type, the native_decide-discharged h11 : ((11:ℕ) : ZMod Secp256k1.p) ≠ 0, the counterexample (p=11 kills the leading coefficient), and all torsion/closure/no-axioms prose_issues (every quoted docstring phrase is verbatim in the file). WRONG: hypothesis item 3 and prose_issue 2 claim Mathlib's natDegree_preΨ' 'needs the domain/nontriviality' provided by the global Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance and that the docstring 'elides' it. At the pinned Mathlib v4.31.0, Degree.lean has only `variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (W : WeierstrassCurve R)` in scope and `lemma natDegree_preΨ' {n : ℕ} (h : (n : R) ≠ 0)` carries no IsDomain/Nontrivial instance ((n:R) ≠ 0 itself forces nontriviality); `ZMod.natCast_eq_zero_iff (a b : ℕ)` (ZMod/Basic.lean:518) has no typeclass hypotheses either. So the docstring 'the only hypothesis is 11 ≠ 0 in 𝔽_p' is type-accurate, and depends_on should drop 'Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean' (it is an import-chain ancestor via DivisionPolynomialDegree, not a logical dependency of this proof). Consequently this theorem's native_decide footprint is only the p ∤ 11 check, not the Pratt certificate.

rows.correction: Accurate except the inheritance claim: the proof inherits only the native_decide dependency from secp256k1_preΨ₁₁_natDegree, NOT a Fact p.Prime dependency (see natDegree row — Mathlib v4.31.0 natDegree_preΨ' needs only [CommRing R] + (n:R) ≠ 0, and the ne_zero proof is intro/rw/norm_num on top of it). exact_type verbatim; the docstring and VERIFIED.md row 155 torsion-gloss criticisms confirmed verbatim; proposed safe rewording is sound.

verify.overall: The audit is substantially accurate and its central adversarial findings all verify against source at 810de34: (1) both files' theorem types are quoted verbatim and prove only F_p polynomial facts (natDegree = 60/84, nonzero, Multiset.card roots ≤ 60/84 over ZMod Secp256k1.p, with multiplicity); (2) the roots→order-ℓ-points transition is asserted in module docstrings, theorem docstrings, theorem NAMES, and VERIFIED.md rows but proved nowhere — grep confirms preΨ' 11/13 occur only in these two files and point-level bridges (secp256k1_{two,three,five,seven}_nsmul_eq_zero_iff) stop at ℓ=7; (3) the advertised #E[11]≤121 / #E[13]≤169 / E[ℓ]≅(ℤ/ℓ)² conclusions live over the algebraic closure while the proved bounds are F_p root counts, and the y≠0 / (x,−y)-distinctness / O-counting steps behind '±y per x' are unformalized; (4) the ledger method column 'Mathlib' on rows 155/156/158/159 omits the inherited native_decide, and 'no axioms' ignores Lean.ofReduceBool; (5) both files are near-identical copies that re-instantiate Mathlib's natDegree_preΨ' instead of the already-merged uniform secp256k1_odd_preΨ_natDegree / secp256k1_odd_torsion_x_card_le (OddTorsionBound.lean, not imported), and the badges bump 209→215 counts six decide-level instantiations as headline theorems; the instance-marker recommendation is sound. The audit's one systematic error over-states a problem: it claims the global Fact (Nat.Prime p) instance is load-bearing for the natDegree and ne_zero theorems and that the docstrings' 'the only hypothesis is 11/13 ≠ 0 in 𝔽_p' elide it. At the pinned Mathlib v4.31.0, natDegree_preΨ' requires only [CommRing R] plus (n:R) ≠ 0 (no IsDomain/Nontrivial), and ZMod.natCast_eq_zero_iff has no typeclass hypotheses — those docstring sentences are type-accurate, and Secp256k1PrimeP is a logical dependency only of the two card_le theorems (whose statements need IsDomain for .roots). Verdicts: rows 3 and 6 CONFIRMED; rows 1, 2, 4, 5 CORRECTED on exactly this Fact-dependency point, all other content in those rows verified."
